Article:
40 Years of Research: A 50% Decline in Male Fertility
The most pronounced and sharpest decline was recorded among men from Western countries • A 52.4% decrease in sperm concentration and a 59.3% decrease in total sperm count
Alarming findings: Over the past 40 years, there has been a continuous downward trend in male fertility in the Western world, with a decline of more than 50 percent in sperm concentration and sperm count. This emerges from an analysis of 7,500 studies published on the subject between 1973 and 2011.
The most notable and steepest decline was observed among men from Western countries. This group consisted of “ordinary” men, not selected based on their fertility status. Among these men, a 52.4% decrease in sperm concentration and a 59.3% decrease in total sperm count were observed over the entire study period. The review included studies from North America, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand. The researchers note that sperm count is not only an important and central measure of male fertility, but also a parameter that may indicate overall health.
Summary: An analysis of 7,500 studies published over the last 40 years shows that there is a concerning and ongoing trend of declining male fertility in the Western world. The data indicates that among these men, there has been a 52.4% decrease in sperm concentration and a 59.3% decrease in total sperm count. It is noted that sperm count is not only an important and central measure of male fertility but also a figure that may indicate his health.

Article: Keeping Calm The summer heat waves are approaching, and the increase in demand for air conditioners is already being felt • Which air conditioner is better? What are the common malfunctions? And what is the average repair cost? And which air conditioner is the technicians' choice? • Guide As the heat rises and temperatures soar, we tend to lower the degrees on the air conditioner - the official savior of summer. As long as everything works and is in order, life is somehow bearable. The nightmare begins when there are malfunctions or if, heaven forbid, the air conditioner does not match the size of the room. The more we use air conditioners, the more likely they are to break down. A chronicle of known malfunctions. Want to receive more updates? Join Israel Hayom on Facebook The Midrag website, which has been ranking professionals since 2003 based on hundreds of thousands of customer reviews in various fields, checked what the most common malfunctions in air conditioners reported by customers are, and what their average repair cost is. Miri Paschansky, the content manager of the Midrag website, reveals that the statistical analysis of the site shows that the most common service is gas refilling in the air conditioner, for which you will pay an average of 443 shekels. "Customers tended to self-diagnose the malfunction when 'the air conditioner is not cooling', and usually blamed the lack of gas. In half of the cases, the problem was indeed a lack of gas, but a third of the cases were resolved by replacing a capacitor or in other ways," says Paschansky. The air conditioning technicians who participated in the survey recommended not to provide a diagnosis of the malfunction but to wait for the air conditioning technician to arrive. The reason is simple - certain technicians may take advantage of the incorrect diagnosis and charge for gas refilling, even though it is not lacking at all. However, there are malfunctions that anyone can fix themselves and do not need to call an air conditioning technician who will charge for a five-minute visit, as the average visit cost of a technician is 250 shekels. Cleaning air conditioner filters, for example, should be done at least twice a year and can prevent future malfunctions. Opening a blockage in the drainage pipe is also a relatively simple action. Before you rush to call a technician, check if the white pipe from which the water drips is blocked and full of dirt. The second most common malfunction among consumers that requires a technician is replacing a compressor capacitor, which costs an average of 361 shekels. There are various other air conditioning malfunctions that can affect the compressor, such as replacing a sensor, repairing drainage pipes, replacing sensors, and more, but they are less common. From the statistical analysis conducted by the Midrag website on customer reviews, you will be pleased to discover that for most malfunctions such as gas refilling, capacitor replacement, leak repair, and more, you will not pay more than 500 shekels. However, when it comes to more complex repairs such as a control board, the price rises and can reach 1,000 shekels or more. The cheapest service is cleaning air conditioner filters, for which customers paid an average of about 276 shekels, while the more expensive and rarer service is repairing a problem in the compressor, which can reach an average of 1,073 shekels. In general, when it comes to an air conditioner less than a decade old, it is advisable to call an air conditioning technician before considering replacing the air conditioner. There is a good chance that the technician will be able to fix the malfunction for a lower amount than it would cost you to buy and install a completely new air conditioner. The Midrag website checked with 30 independent air conditioning technicians, who were rated by thousands of customers as the best technicians, which air conditioner they recommend for an average Israeli family looking for a reliable air conditioner at a reasonable price. The technicians were asked to provide a first and second preference and to refer only to the reliability of the air conditioner, which is considered one with relatively few malfunctions, and even when there is a problem, it is easy to fix. 48% of the technicians unanimously recommended that for a family with no price limit, an air conditioner from Mitsubishi is the most reliable. Among air conditioners with a reasonable price for an average family, Electra's air conditioners were ranked first as the most reliable, with about half of the technicians' votes. In second place, 67% of the technicians chose Tadiran's air conditioners, and in third place, Tornado's air conditioners. In the Consumer Council, which works to change the behavioral rules of businesses and public bodies in Israel and to instill an economic culture based on fairness that benefits the public, the government, and businesses alike, they are well aware of the public complaints about air conditioning companies. The organization's center receives hundreds of complaints each year about the air conditioning industry. Most of them, not surprisingly, during the summer. Merav Cohen, the organization's CEO, compares the common complaints in the air conditioning industry to other fields of electrical and electronic devices with one significant difference: "In the air conditioning field, there is a prominent phenomenon of installation failures, which have implications for the air conditioner's electricity consumption and sometimes also on the frequency of malfunctions in the air conditioner's operation." Cohen explains that "there are many air conditioning installers in the market who lack training as electricians and professional familiarity with the air conditioners they seek to install. The Consumer Council is currently examining ways to assist the public in distinguishing between a professional air conditioning installer and one who is not. For now, at least, consumers should ensure that the installer they are hiring is a certified electrician." An analysis of the complaints received by the organization shows that most of them, about 20%, concern poor technical service. One complainant, for example, called a technician due to a faulty air conditioner, who determined that the control board needed to be replaced. The visit cost the customer 250 shekels, but after about a week, the technician came to replace the board and charged an additional 550 shekels. After a few days, the air conditioner broke down again. When a technician arrived again, he determined that the compressor needed to be replaced at a cost of 2,500 shekels. This "waste" of money could have been avoided if the first technician had conducted a thorough examination, identified the real problem, and solved it on the spot. What type of air conditioner should you choose? Another common complaint, accounting for about 18% of the total complaints, is the supply of defective products. Many complaints are received about new air conditioners that are not functioning properly from day one, leaking, making noise, and more. Poor customer service accounts for about 18% of the dubious complaint pie. There is no need to elaborate on this issue, as there is hardly anyone who has not encountered poor service in everyday life. Problems with technicians account for 17% of the complaints, including technicians who were eight and a half hours late, unprofessional technicians, and more. 8% of the complaints relate to customers' difficulties in exercising their warranty. People buy air conditioners, and while under warranty, companies try to shirk and find loopholes so they do not have to bear the costs of replacements and repairs. Recurring and multiple malfunctions account for 3% of the total complaints, while misleading customers, difficulties in canceling transactions, delays in repairs and service, and all other problems that customers complain about account for 13% of the shame pie. Beyond malfunctions and complaints, it turns out that choosing which air conditioner to place in each room is not a simple task at all. In the past, this dilemma did not exist. It was clear that in a small room we would install a window air conditioner and in the living room, we would install a larger air conditioner. Today, many more variables are taken into account. Everyone we spoke to in the field, in all companies, recommended first and foremost that an expert come to the site and recommend the most suitable air conditioner. This is due to the multitude of solutions available in the field of room cooling. An installer or air conditioning contractor will understand exactly the need and can provide the best response. Since Willis Carrier invented the air conditioner in 1906 and coined the term "air conditioning", the air conditioner has come a long way. Its well-known models, some of which still star in every hot corner of the country today, are window air conditioners or split air conditioners. A window air conditioner is a single unit fixed to an external wall, with an internal part and an external part. These are old and noisy air conditioners whose installation is difficult, and therefore they are hardly used today, even though they are relatively cheap. Split air conditioners, on the other hand, are the vast majority, if not all, of the air conditioners marketed today. Inside the house is the cooling unit (or units), and outside the house is the compressor unit. The split allows for quiet operation and efficiency in heat removal, but requires the transfer of cables and pipes between the units. A split air conditioner can come in several configurations: wall-mounted, floor-mounted, or mini-central. A wall-mounted air conditioner, as its name suggests, is an air conditioner located high on the room wall, near the ceiling, and blows cold air downwards. Such cooling reaches all parts of the room quickly, but it is not necessarily more efficient in terms of the ratio of energy consumption to cooling output. The main disadvantage of wall-mounted air conditioners is in heating, which is less efficient; floor-mounted air conditioners are larger and located closer to the floor, and their air outlet is usually located at the top. The cooling process is longer with such an air conditioner, and it takes up more space, but its cooling efficiency is very high and it is considered more efficient in heating; a mini-central air conditioner is a very common solution in large apartments, private homes, and new construction in general. As with any split air conditioner, the compressor unit is located outside the house. The cooling (and heating) unit is installed in a central location in the apartment, usually above a lowered ceiling in a corridor or bathroom, and blows air through a duct system to all rooms in the house. The control of the air flow is done through shutters and dampers in the rooms. The major advantages of a mini-central air conditioner are cooling the entire apartment and low noise levels in the rooms. The disadvantages are the structural requirements for its installation and the lack of adaptation to the different requirements in different rooms. There are many parameters to consider when choosing the type of air conditioner; where the apartment is located, the directions of the walls and windows, whether the air-conditioned area is part of a shared or private house, the type of roof and ceiling, the number of people in the house, additional heat sources, and more. These are exactly the considerations that professionals take into account when recommending which air conditioner is suitable for which space. New air conditioners operate on a method called "inverter", in which, unlike older air conditioners, the air conditioner's compressor operates continuously, reducing the need to turn the air conditioner off and on, which consumes a lot of electricity and causes rapid and significant wear. The air conditioner actually adjusts itself to the room temperature and keeps the room at a constant temperature as long as it operates. This method saves 30%-40% of electricity consumption. Another significant contribution is the much lower wear on these air conditioners. The compressor operates at a reasonable speed range and does not overexert itself, the temperature regulation method maintains a state of continuous savings, and after only an hour or two of cooling the room, the inverter enters a savings mode, allowing the air conditioner to operate continuously and maintain the compressor. No matter which air conditioner you choose, to get the best output and performance from the air conditioner, it is recommended to maintain it regularly throughout the year by performing a few simple actions - the air filters should be cleaned regularly; in the summer days, the temperature should be set to 20-24 degrees Celsius (yes, even on the hottest days) and the air vents should be directed parallel to the floor or slightly upward; if the outdoor unit is installed on the ground, make sure its surroundings are clean, so that no weeds, plastic bags, or any other dirt is sucked into it; and most importantly, you should keep your fingers crossed that the air conditioner will survive its many hours of operation and withstand the heat load and effort. If there are going to be malfunctions, it's better during the transitional seasons. A pleasant and reasonable summer to all of us.
Summary: With the summer heat loads and the increase in the use of air conditioners, malfunctions also rise, and this guide reviews common malfunctions, repair costs, and recommendations for choosing an air conditioner. Common malfunctions in air conditioners include gas shortage, faulty compressor capacitor, clogging in the drainage pipe, and more. It is important to remember that there are simple malfunctions that can be fixed by oneself, such as cleaning filters or unclogging the drainage pipe. According to a survey conducted among technicians, Mitsubishi and Electra brands are considered particularly reliable. The Consumer Trust Organization reports many complaints regarding poor service in the air conditioning industry, such as inadequate technical service and the supply of defective products. Before choosing an air conditioner, it is recommended to consult with an expert to select the most suitable air conditioner for your needs, taking into account the size of the space, the location of the apartment, the type of ceiling, and more. Today, most new air conditioners operate on inverter technology, which allows for continuous and more energy-efficient operation compared to older air conditioners. To ensure the air conditioner's efficiency over time, it is important to perform regular maintenance that includes cleaning filters, setting the correct temperature, and keeping the area around the outdoor unit clean.
Sentence in Summary: According to a survey conducted among technicians, Mitsubishi and Electra brands are considered particularly reliable
Best Match Sentences From Article: 48% of the technicians unanimously recommended that for a family with no price limit, an air conditioner from Mitsubishi is the most reliable

Article:
The number of tests has increased, but the percentage of positives has decreased

815 new confirmed cases of COVID-19 were identified yesterday in 56,512 tests • The rate of confirmed cases - 1.5% • Prof. Levy in a radio interview: "We are preparing for COVID-19 tests for students"

The Ministry of Health published the updated COVID-19 morbidity data, according to which yesterday (Tuesday) 815 new confirmed cases were identified in 56,512 tests conducted. Therefore, the percentage of positives excluding tests aimed at identifying recoveries stood at 1.5. The downward trend continues, as the rate of diagnosed cases the day before yesterday was 1.8% and the day before that a negative record of 2.7% was registered in recent times.

In Israel, there are 8,310 active patients. The total number of recoveries as of 07:34 stands at 325,862. The number of deaths since the outbreak of the pandemic stands at 2,735.

309 people are hospitalized in serious condition. The condition of 134 of them is critical and 132 are connected to ventilators. The condition of 76 individuals is defined as moderate.

In Jerusalem, 1,086 active patients were recorded, in Nazareth 363, in Tel Aviv-Yafo 325, in the local council of Buq'ata 266, in Haifa 222, and in Bnei Brak there are 200 carriers of the virus as of today.

"In recent days, an increase in the number of new confirmed cases has been identified (part of which can be attributed to an increase in the number of tests), for the first time in about a month and a half," it was noted in the daily report of the information and knowledge center. "The continuation of the easing without excessive adherence to accompany them (masks, social distancing, enforcement, etc.) is expected to raise the level of morbidity".

In this context, the Director General of the Ministry of Health, Prof. Hazi Levy, said in an interview with 103FM: "We have started to prepare for COVID-19 tests for school students. There are technical and legal difficulties regarding this matter".

Contributed to the preparation of the news: Meital Yasar Beit-Or
Summary: The Ministry of Health published the updated COVID-19 morbidity data: Yesterday (Tuesday), 815 new confirmed cases were identified out of 56,512 tests conducted. Article:
The number of tests has increased, but the percentage of positives has decreased

815 new confirmed cases of COVID-19 were identified yesterday in 56,512 tests • The rate of confirmed cases - 1.5% • Prof. Levy in a radio interview: "We are preparing for COVID-19 tests for students"

The Ministry of Health published the updated COVID-19 morbidity data, according to which yesterday (Tuesday) 815 new confirmed cases were identified in 56,512 tests conducted. Therefore, the percentage of positives excluding tests aimed at identifying recoveries stood at 1.5. The downward trend continues, as the rate of diagnosed cases the day before yesterday was 1.8% and the day before that a negative record of 2.7% was registered in recent times.

In Israel, there are 8,310 active patients. The total number of recoveries as of 07:34 stands at 325,862. The number of deaths since the outbreak of the pandemic stands at 2,735.

309 people are hospitalized in serious condition. The condition of 134 of them is critical and 132 are connected to ventilators. The condition of 76 individuals is defined as moderate.

In Jerusalem, 1,086 active patients were recorded, in Nazareth 363, in Tel Aviv-Yafo 325, in the local council of Buq'ata 266, in Haifa 222, and in Bnei Brak there are 200 carriers of the virus as of today.

"In recent days, an increase in the number of new confirmed cases has been identified (part of which can be attributed to an increase in the number of tests), for the first time in about a month and a half," it was noted in the daily report of the information and knowledge center. "The continuation of the easing without excessive adherence to accompany them (masks, social distancing, enforcement, etc.) is expected to raise the level of morbidity".

In this context, the Director General of the Ministry of Health, Prof. Hazi Levy, said in an interview with 103FM: "We have started to prepare for COVID-19 tests for school students. There are technical and legal difficulties regarding this matter".

The condition of 134 of them is critical and 132 are connected to ventilators. The condition of 76 individuals is defined as moderate.

Article: Teva workers are barricading in Jerusalem: "We want to be thrown out to the street" The general strike that ended was accompanied by a protest of hundreds of Teva workers • In Jerusalem, the workers are barricading at the Teva factory • The Prime Minister and the Minister of Finance will meet later today with the CEO of Teva after blocking the main routes in Jerusalem and other locations across the country, hundreds of Teva workers are currently barricading at the company's factories in Jerusalem. The Histadrut stated that: "The workers' cry will continue to resonate until the walls of indifference are broken. The workers are not invisible and the Histadrut will continue to fight for them and alongside them until a suitable solution to the crisis is formulated." Meanwhile, we remind that tomorrow at 10:00 AM, Histadrut Chairman Avi Nissenkorn, Finance Minister Moshe Kahlon, and Minister of Economy and Industry Eli Cohen are expected to hold a special meeting with the workers and with the heads of the committees at Teva. Alongside the massive strike that took place this morning (Sunday) across the country, hundreds of Teva workers from various factories protested this morning (Sunday) from north to south in a show of protest against the massive layoffs in the company. During the protests this morning, the Golda Meir - Har Hotzvim intersection was blocked to traffic by several dozen demonstrators. Hundreds of Teva headquarters employees in Petah Tikva blocked the Jabotinsky route in the city – one of the most central roads in the Gush Dan area. In Kfar Saba, the workers went out to protest, but no roadblocks were reported from there. The Jerusalem police reported that Teva workers protested at the Ruppin - Kaplan intersection (the clock), they set tires on fire which were quickly extinguished by security forces. Kaplan - Zusman Street was blocked to traffic for a short time. Some of the demonstrators also blocked the Shazar-Yirmiyahu intersection. Later, the demonstrators reached the String Bridge at the entrance to the city and blocked traffic. In Ashdod, dozens of Teva Medical factory workers protested. The workers carried signs saying "Do not close a vital factory" and "Vigodman, return the money, shame on you" and also "We want to be at work" "Good at firing employees". As a result of the protest, traffic congestion was created in the industrial area, especially on the way to the factory on HaOrguim Street. At one point, several workers set tires on fire and police forces intervened. Several drivers whose way was blocked confronted the demonstrators. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Finance Minister Moshe Kahlon are expected to meet with the CEO of the pharmaceutical giant, Karl Schultz, immediately upon his landing in the country, already today. In the meeting, which is also expected to include Economy Minister Eli Cohen, Netanyahu and Kahlon will present a united front - and a clear demand from Teva: to lay off more employees abroad and significantly reduce the severity of the decree in Israel. This is based on the understanding that the company's headquarters will continue to operate in the country. In a meeting of his ministers, Netanyahu decided to establish a ministerial team (PM, Chaim Katz, Elkin, Eli Cohen) to deal with the crisis at Teva. The Prime Minister asked the ministers not to act through different and uncoordinated channels regarding the Teva crisis and set a discussion for tomorrow with the aforementioned ministers in order to formulate a unified government plan. Finance Minister Kahlon said in closed conversations since the announcement of the mass layoffs that the government intends to minimize the extent of the layoffs in Israel as much as possible and to provide an effective response to those who will be sent home. "For decades, the state has helped Teva. Now the eyes of the workers are turned to us," said the Finance Minister to his people. Tomorrow morning (Monday), Finance Minister Moshe Kahlon and Economy Minister Eli Cohen, together with Histadrut Chairman Avi Nissenkorn and Yoav Shimhi, Chairman of the National Histadrut, will meet with the heads of Teva's workers' organizations at the Ministry of Finance in Jerusalem. The workers' committees at Teva announced that "we will carry out the hardest move that Teva has not known since its establishment 116 years ago. The workers' committees and the Histadrut will close and lock the iron gates at the factory in Kfar Saba - thus announcing the beginning of the great strike and solidarity with our friends at the various Teva sites." Alongside the strike, protests and protest vigils will be held at several locations across the country. In a message to all employees, a threat was published on behalf of the committee, stating that "the committee suggests to anyone who intends to come to avoid unnecessary inconveniences." The employees at the company's offices in Netanya are furious at the management's instruction to its managers not to come to the offices today. "What are they afraid of, that we will lynch them? I want them to look me in the eyes and tell me what I can do at the age of 55?" says Chaim, a resident of the city who came to protest with his children. "Where can my friends and I go from here? We will have to shut down the company until someone wakes up and decides to take the initiative and talk to all the relevant parties to solve this crisis and save the situation. I have been here for over 30 years and I do not intend to end my life here. Where is the Histadrut? Why has it not yet held a massive protest to try to prevent the situation?" Daniel Shabtai, Chairman of the Workers' Committee at Teva Petah Tikva, has worked at Teva for 18 years in the company's computer unit. "The people here work shifts around the clock. They have given their soul, their lives - like me. I got married here and I had 4 children here. This situation is sad and very frustrating. It is very disappointing that we have reached this situation, disappointing that those responsible for this situation are not involved at any cost and do not care - and I mean the previous management and the current board members who approved this draconian proposal without batting an eye. We must be given answers about what is happening. No one is giving us answers." "The fight here is not only for us at Teva, but also for a similar company in Israel that will be in a helpless situation like us. As of now, the managers were instructed today to stay home and not come to the office. What were they afraid of? That people would speak to their hearts and tell about their concerns and show their frustration and fears in their eyes?" Meanwhile, the Mayor of Be'er Sheva, Rubik Danilovich, is expected to convene today the heads of local authorities in the Negev for an emergency meeting, as part of a public struggle against the intention to close the Teva factory in Neot Hovav, where more than a thousand workers are employed. The workers themselves are also expected to arrive at the meeting, in a march that will leave from the factory gates. Danilovich noted that they intend to hold urgent meetings as soon as possible with the Prime Minister, the Finance Minister, and the Minister of Economy. The preparation of the report was participated by: Ze'ev Klein, Gadi Golan, Shlomi Diaz, Amatzia Samakai, and Mital Yasar Beit-Or.
Summary: Hundreds of Teva employees are barricading themselves in the company's factories in Jerusalem in protest of the expected mass layoffs. The workers are protesting the management's intention to lay off many employees in Israel, while demanding a much more significant reduction of the workforce abroad. Throughout the day, Teva employees held demonstrations across the country, during which major roads were blocked. Meanwhile, Teva employees in Netanya are furious that the company's management instructed its managers not to come to the offices today and are demanding explanations from the management regarding the conduct. Daniel Shabtai, chairman of the workers' committee at Teva Petah Tikva, expressed deep disappointment with the situation and called on the previous management and current board members to provide answers to the employees. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Finance Minister Moshe Kahlon are expected to meet today with Teva CEO, Kare Schultz, to discuss the crisis. Finance Minister Kahlon emphasized that the government intends to reduce the scope of layoffs in Israel and provide an appropriate response to the employees who will be laid off. Tomorrow, another discussion will take place at the Ministry of Finance in Jerusalem, with the participation of the Finance Minister, the Minister of Economy, the chairman of the Histadrut, and representatives of Teva employees. Meanwhile, the mayor of Be'er Sheva, Rubik Danilovich, will convene today the heads of local authorities in the Negev for an emergency conference, aiming to prevent the closure of the Teva factory in Neot Hovav; the conference will also include factory employees, who will march from the factory gates. Danilovich noted that they intend to hold urgent meetings with the Prime Minister, Finance Minister, and Minister of Economy.
Sentence in Summary: Finance Minister Kahlon emphasized that the government intends to reduce the scope of layoffs in Israel and provide an appropriate response to the employees who will be laid off

Article:
The Days of Innocence: Childhood Games Are Back in Fashion

After exhausting computer games, TV series, and virtual activities, children are rediscovering the games "of old" • Rope, stones, and sticks - nostalgia is making a big comeback • All the games for home and yard

Spring 2020 will be remembered in our history books as the spring of Corona, lockdown, and furloughs, but also as a time when we learned to slow down, turn inward, discover what is within a hundred meters of our home, get to know the kitchen up close, and mainly - spend entire weeks with our beloved children at home, searching for new ideas for activities.

As part of these creative solutions, our childhood games have recently returned to us - the generation of parents who did not know the internet, tablets, iPhones, and Xbox, and were content with street games with other children. Our "play tools" then included rubber, rope, sticks, chalk, a sidewalk stone, five tiny stones, and a ball, and this simplicity managed to fill us with great joy and happiness, not to mention the energy expenditure and physical activity that were part of it.

It is hard not to notice that recently, joyful street childhood sounds are emerging from the yards and sidewalks, within a hundred meters, those sounds that in the last decade have made way for the sounds of ringtones and TikToks at home. Thus, our sophisticated Generation Z children encounter those forgotten and beloved childhood games of ours, which, under the auspices of Corona, are experiencing a revival as part of intergenerational "bonding."

We have gathered some of these popular childhood games, suitable for two or three participants and also for larger groups, suitable for larger families and/or allowing parental participation. Some of the games are more suitable for the yard, while others are also suitable for indoors. For the indoor games presented here, we consulted with Shlomi Aylon, operator of "Neighborhood Games in the Garden." Aylon adapted some of the childhood games for indoor play, and also for younger children, who in some of the games will benefit from developing muscles related to fine motor skills. And if you don't quite remember the rules, you can always find videos online that will refresh your memory. Enjoy!

At Home:

Five Stones

For us, these were five square metal cubes, but if you don't have those, you can also play with five pebbles. Each player takes turns holding the stones in one hand, then spreads them on the floor. Now they need to collect them, throwing one stone in the air each time, and while it is in the air, they should pick up a stone from the floor until all the stones return to their hand. In the next stage, they collect two stones together while one stone is thrown in the air, and in the next stage, they collect three stones together from the floor. The game increases in difficulty with various options that you can recall from memory or search for in videos online. For younger ages, there is a simpler variation: place one stone on the back of the hand and spread the other four on the floor, without letting the stone on the back fall. Now, they need to collect the stones again, again without letting the stone on the back of the hand fall. Again, the difficulty level increases, starting with collecting each stone separately, then every two together, etc.

Black Finger

A family game that is especially suitable for little ones. Sit on the carpet in a circle, with one child sitting in the center, eyes closed. The game leader, chosen in advance, decides who among the participants will touch the child sitting in the center with their finger, while everyone around simultaneously calls out "buzz" and waves their fingers in the air. The child in the center must guess who among the surrounding players touched them.

Jumping Rope

A variation of the jump rope game, adapted for younger children and also allows for indoor play. One participant holds one end of a rope that is laid on the floor. They move their hand quickly back and forth so that the rope curves like a snake on the floor. The little children, each in turn, jump outside the rope without it touching them.

Gogos

While we used to play in the yard with gogos, those pits of fruit seeds we collected, trying to throw them into a hole in the ground, the indoor variation of the game is called "Gogo Close to the Wall" and involves sitting on the floor in a straight line about three meters from the wall. Each child is given an equal number of seeds. Each child holds one seed between their index finger and thumb, and using their finger, they flick it on the floor, as far as possible towards the wall. Whoever gets closest to the wall collects the pile of seeds from the others. This is repeated several times, and the winner is the one who ends up with the largest number of gogos. Of course, care must be taken that the seeds do not reach toddlers, who might swallow them.

Gogo Football

The children sit on the floor about a meter apart from each other. One creates a kind of "goal" with their finger and thumb vertical to the floor, and the other must shoot the gogo in their hand into the goal. Again, the grip on the gogo is between the thumb and finger (as we would play with marbles), with the gogo sliding to the floor towards the goal using the finger that is sent over it. And again, be careful with seeds in the hands of toddlers.

In the Yard:

Jumping Rope

Two participants stand facing each other about two to three meters apart, each holding one end of a rope that is about four to five meters long. They spin the rope in one direction, and the other participants, each in turn, must jump through the rope without touching it. Anyone who fails is disqualified and switches places with one of the rope spinners. The one who manages to jump the most times in a row without being disqualified is the winner.

Hopscotch

In the past, many sidewalks were decorated with drawings of the hopscotch game, which were outlined on them with a chalk stone. Three vertical squares, followed by two horizontal ones (side by side) and one square above, and two horizontal squares above it (eight squares in total), and above this impressive structure, a half-circle was drawn, called "sky." The squares are marked with numbers from 1 to 8 from bottom to top. The playing tool is some flat stone, which the first player throws onto the first square. Now they hop to the square on one foot, stick their foot to the stone, and then must drag it with a precise kick to the next square. They must not step on the lines or throw the stone outside the squares. When they reach the pair of horizontal squares, they jump on them with both feet, with each foot on a different square - and this creates the memorable hopscotch jump. When they reach the half-circle, they lift the stone with their hand and jump in a turn so that they are facing the first square, and this time they return to it only by jumping and without dragging the stone. If the stone does not land in the correct square or is thrown on the line, the turn passes to the next participant. In the next round, each participant continues from the square where they were disqualified in the previous round.

Jumping Rubber

Two participants stand facing each other with a slight spread, about two meters apart. They thread a rubber band about four meters long through their legs, with both ends tied together, so that the rubber creates a rectangle between them that is as long as the distance between them and as wide as the spread between their legs. They start with the rubber at ankle height. The other participants, each in turn, must jump between the two rows of rubber in various ways (if you don't remember how, you can watch explanatory videos online). To increase the difficulty level, raise the rubber to mid-calf height, and then to knee height.

Twenty-One

The participants stand in a circle and pass a ball among themselves, while one participant chosen in advance stands among them and tries to snatch the ball from them or touch it and make it fall to the floor. The goal is to make 21 consecutive passes without the ball being snatched or falling to the floor.

One, Two, Three, Salty Fish

One of the participants is the "salty fish," and they stand with their eyes closed and back to the other participants, who stand behind a line at a distance from them. While they close their eyes and cannot see, the other participants move quickly towards them. The "salty fish" must say "one, two, three, salty fish" and then turn to the participants and look at them. They are supposed to freeze in place but also try to touch them from behind without being noticed. If the "salty fish" sees someone moving, they command them to return to the starting line, and if one of the participants manages to reach the "salty fish" and touch their back without being caught in motion, the "salty fish" must tell a story to all the children. During the story, they must mention the word "salty fish," and then everyone must run back behind the starting line. Anyone caught by the "salty fish" before reaching the line becomes the "salty fish" in the next round. The indoor variation of the game for toddlers includes crawling or walking on knees instead of running.

Color Tag

Choose who will be the tagger, and the other participants scatter around them. The tagger chooses a specific color and announces it aloud, and the participants must hurry to touch an object or clothing item that is of the chosen color. The tagger must catch anyone who fails to touch the color, and that person becomes the tagger in the next round. If the tagger fails to catch such a participant, they continue to be the tagger and announce another color.

Three Sticks

Arrange three sticks at equal distances. The goal is to jump between the sticks in just three steps (one step between each gap), without moving any stick from its place. Start with a small gap between the sticks, and after everyone succeeds in jumping between the sticks, increase the distance between them. Anyone who fails to jump in one step between each pair of sticks is disqualified, and the winner is the last one remaining without being disqualified.
Summary: After exhausting computer games, television series, and virtual activities, children are discovering the games "of old" that are coming back into fashion. Spring 2020 will be remembered in our history books as the spring of Corona, lockdown, and furlough, but also as a time when we learned to slow down, discover what is within a hundred meters of our home, get to know the kitchen up close, and mainly - spend entire weeks at home searching for new ideas for activities. As part of these creative solutions, childhood games from the parents' generation, who did not know the internet, tablets, iPhones, and Xbox, have recently returned to us, and they were satisfied with street games with other children. The "play tools" then included rubber, rope, sticks, chalk, a curb stone, five tiny stones, and a ball. The author of the article collected some of these popular childhood games, suitable for two or three participants and also for a larger number, suitable for larger families and/or allowing parental participation. Among them is the snake game where one participant holds one end of a rope placed on the floor. They move their hand quickly to the right and left so that the rope curves like a snake on the floor, and each in turn jumps outside the rope without touching it. Another game is three sticks. Three sticks are arranged at equal distances, and the goal is to jump between the sticks in only three steps (one step between each gap), without moving any stick from its place. They start with a small gap between the sticks, and after everyone succeeds in jumping between the sticks, they increase the distance between them. Anyone who fails to jump in one step between each pair of sticks is disqualified, and the winner is the last one remaining without being disqualified.
Sentence in Summary: Three sticks are arranged at equal distances, and the goal is to jump between the sticks in only three steps (one step between each gap), without moving any stick from its place

One of the participants is the "salty fish," and they stand with their eyes closed and back to the other participants, who stand behind a line at a distance from them. While they close their eyes and cannot see, the other participants move quickly towards them. The "salty fish" must say "one, two, three, salty fish" and then turn to the participants and look at them. They are supposed to freeze in place but also try to touch them from behind without being noticed. If the "salty fish" sees someone moving, they command them to return to the starting line, and if one of the participants manages to reach the "salty fish" and touch their back without being caught in motion, the "salty fish" must tell a story to all the children. During the story, they must mention the word "salty fish," and then everyone must run back behind the starting line. Anyone caught by the "salty fish" before reaching the line becomes the "salty fish" in the next round. The indoor variation of the game for toddlers includes crawling or walking on knees instead of running.

Color Tag

Choose who will be the tagger, and the other participants scatter around them. The tagger chooses a specific color and announces it aloud, and the participants must hurry to touch an object or clothing item that is of the chosen color. The tagger must catch anyone who fails to touch the color, and that person becomes the tagger in the next round. If the tagger fails to catch such a participant, they continue to be the tagger and announce another color.

Three Sticks

Arrange three sticks at equal distances. The goal is to jump between the sticks in just three steps (one step between each gap), without moving any stick from its place. Start with a small gap between the sticks, and after everyone succeeds in jumping between the sticks, increase the distance between them. Anyone who fails to jump in one step between each pair of sticks is disqualified, and the winner is the last one remaining without being disqualified.
Summary: After exhausting computer games, television series, and virtual activities, children are discovering the games "of old" that are coming back into fashion. Spring 2020 will be remembered in our history books as the spring of Corona, lockdown, and furlough, but also as a time when we learned to slow down, discover what is within a hundred meters of our home, get to know the kitchen up close, and mainly - spend entire weeks at home searching for new ideas for activities. As part of these creative solutions, childhood games from the parents' generation, who did not know the internet, tablets, iPhones, and Xbox, have recently returned to us, and they were satisfied with street games with other children. The "play tools" then included rubber, rope, sticks, chalk, a curb stone, five tiny stones, and a ball. The author of the article collected some of these popular childhood games, suitable for two or three participants and also for a larger number, suitable for larger families and/or allowing parental participation. Among them is the snake game where one participant holds one end of a rope placed on the floor. They move their hand quickly to the right and left so that the rope curves like a snake on the floor, and each in turn jumps outside the rope without touching it. Another game is three sticks. Three sticks are arranged at equal distances, and the goal is to jump between the sticks in only three steps (one step between each gap), without moving any stick from its place. They start with a small gap between the sticks, and after everyone succeeds in jumping between the sticks, they increase the distance between them. Anyone who fails to jump in one step between each pair of sticks is disqualified, and the winner is the last one remaining without being disqualified.

Article: No new parking standard in central Tel Aviv: one parking space for every two apartments. A check on the Yad2 website reflects the demand and distress in Tel Aviv just like in New York, London, or Singapore; parking is becoming a sought-after and expensive commodity, almost like a house. The price of a parking space in the heart of Tel Aviv is today equivalent to a 3-room apartment in Be'er Sheva. In Manhattan, a parking space has already been sold for a million dollars. And in Israel? Slowly but surely, we are on our way there. About two weeks ago, the local planning and building committee of the Tel Aviv municipality approved an updated parking standard for future city building plans. In the city center, the maximum parking standard will now stand at 0.5 parking spaces for each unit. That is, one parking space for every two apartments. In the northern and southern parts of the city, the maximum standard will be 0.8. This means that every second new apartment built in the city center will be sold without an attached parking space. How will this affect parking prices? Already today, parking in Tel Aviv is a rare commodity for both residents and visitors or workers. According to data from the Yad2 website, parking prices in Tel Aviv reflect the high demand from residents and visitors. Parking spaces for sale range from 140,000 to 550,000 shekels, and rental prices range from 350 shekels per month (4,200 shekels per year) to 1,250 shekels per month (15,000 shekels per year), depending on the neighborhood. Parking spaces are a profitable business in every respect, with an average yield of 0.3%-0.4% from rentals and a return ranging from 6% to 13% of the apartment's value when selling the parking space. When examining prices by neighborhoods, in the heart of the city north (Ahad Ha'am / Balfour streets), the price of a parking space for purchase is the highest, reaching about 550,000 shekels. In other streets in the same neighborhood, the price stands at 500,000 shekels. Parking prices in the Neve Tzedek neighborhood stand at 450,000 shekels, and the return from the sale is 9% of the apartment's value. In the Kerem HaTeimanim neighborhood, the price is similar, and the return is 10%. In Ramat Aviv G, parking prices range from 250,000 to 400,000 shekels, and the return ranges from 7% to 10%. The cost of parking in Tel Aviv currently averages about 35,000 shekels per square meter, much more than the price per square meter in apartments in most cities in the country. More cars, less parking According to Haim Masilati, chairman of the appraisers' association, "In recent years, there has been a trend where, on the one hand, there are fewer parking spaces in Tel Aviv, especially in the center, and on the other hand, there are more cars. There are areas characterized by young families, where each family has more than one car. As the distress increases, so does the demand, and with it the price. In the north and south of Tel Aviv, the parking distress is less felt, and therefore the prices are less expensive than in the city center." Masilati estimates that the parking distress will significantly increase due to the reduction of parking standards in new residential projects and emphasizes that the numbers mentioned represent a maximum limit, meaning that a lower standard can even be set. In 2018, parking spaces in employment, commercial, educational, cultural, and leisure projects were reduced. The closer the project is to public transport hubs, the lower the standard. "Parking prices may rise by tens of percent until a suitable public transport infrastructure is established, which will provide a worthy alternative to private vehicles," says Masilati. Another parameter that affects the price is the type of parking: open, covered, and underground parking, which are much more convenient and priced higher. There is also variability in parking within parking garages, for example, according to proximity to the elevator and exit. The gap between quality parking inside an underground garage and parking in a less attractive location outside it can reach 10% or more. "Parking prices are expected to continue to rise in the coming years," estimates Masilati, "at least until the aforementioned transport infrastructure, such as the light rail and metro, provides an alternative solution to the public and encourages people to give up the use of private vehicles." Problematic access in the short term According to attorney Anat Biran, an expert in planning and construction, the current tendency is to reduce parking standards in both residential and office areas. "The rationale is that thanks to accessibility to public transport, there will be no need for a private vehicle. This is a problematic approach in the short and medium term, as it is still unclear whether and when the metro and future light rail lines will begin to operate." The implication of reducing the parking standard in Tel Aviv, she says, is that many residents will not be able to own a car. "This is a problematic situation for residents in a reality where there is no good public transport, as well as for developers who have been working on plans for years and suddenly need to change them according to the new parking standards and make economic assessments accordingly. "There are authorities like Herzliya and Givatayim that require a high parking standard, underground parking that requires descending to several basement floors, and so on. The mismatch between the reduction of parking spaces in employment areas and the increased demand for parking spaces in cities outside Tel Aviv creates a real problem for private vehicle users, who do not have parking available near their workplaces. "All of these, along with the slowness and uncertainty regarding the implementation of public transport plans, require planning neighborhoods that allow for creative, convenient, and safe transportation solutions for pedestrians alongside bike paths, and innovative solutions for working from home or near home, in a way that will reduce transportation problems." Rethinking the route Meital Lahav, Deputy Mayor of Tel Aviv-Yafo and holder of the transportation portfolio, explains that the proposal to define a reduced parking standard in the city aligns with the municipality's policy "to turn the traffic pyramid upside down, to prioritize pedestrians and reduce the use of private vehicles in the city." "This is a proposal derived from the congested reality we live in, which is expected to become even more congested with the implementation of light rail lines, additional transport routes, and bike paths, which will reduce the capacity of the roads in the city and its entrances," says Lahav. "This, alongside the increase in urban density, requires us to rethink the route and make difficult decisions regarding the allocation of urban space." Lahav details various actions that the municipality is promoting in the field of parking, including limiting the distribution of parking permits, differential pricing of parking on streets, regulating a mechanism for short-term parking on commercial streets, alternative solutions for park-and-ride lots at the city entrances, expanding responses for shared mobility, regulating rules for mobility with personal vehicles, and developing infrastructure for safe cycling, implementing additional public transport routes, and raising the level of service for passengers, encouraging the deployment of service taxis, and continuing to operate initiatives like Naim BaSofash, whose goal is to provide transportation solutions 24/7.
Summary: The Tel Aviv municipality approved a new parking standard according to which, in the city center, at most one parking space will be allocated for every two new apartments. The new standard will lead to a reduction in the number of parking spaces in these areas, which is expected to affect existing parking prices. Already today, the high demand for parking in Tel Aviv is evident in high rental and sale prices. Experts estimate that the reduction of parking spaces in new projects will lead to a significant increase in existing parking prices, especially in the city center. The logic behind the move is to encourage the use of public transportation, but there are concerns regarding its practicality in the short and medium term. The Tel Aviv municipality is promoting various actions in the parking field, including limiting parking permits, differential pricing, park-and-ride solutions, and expanding public transportation options. The goal of the move is to reduce the use of private cars and make Tel Aviv a more pedestrian and bicycle-friendly city. Experts estimate that parking prices will continue to rise until public transportation infrastructure is a suitable alternative to private cars. There is a significant gap between parking prices in different places in Tel Aviv, with underground and covered parking being more expensive than open parking. According to data from the Yad2 website, parking prices for sale in Tel Aviv range from 140,000 shekels to 550,000 shekels, depending on the area. Parking rental prices in Tel Aviv range from 350 shekels per month to 1,250 shekels per month, depending on the location. The situation requires creative transportation solutions, such as bike lanes, working from home, and encouraging shared transportation.
Sentence in Summary: The Tel Aviv municipality approved a new parking standard according to which, in the city center, at most one parking space will be allocated for every two new apartments
Best Match Sentences From Article: About two weeks ago, the local planning and building committee of the Tel Aviv municipality approved an updated parking standard for future city building plans

Article: Who Moved My Cheese? Cheesecake, the undisputed queen of Shavuot, in three versions that add flavor and color • Italian tiramisu with a coffee aroma, French fraise with strawberry, and a cold cake with chocolate coating • Happy holiday! In honor of Shavuot, the holiday of cheeses, I am sharing three recipes for festive cakes that are beautiful and have an extraordinary appearance and taste. Shavuot in the food section: Miki Shamo with five dairy dishes that we all love the most. A basket of cheeses from the market: Maya Drin with two winning recipes for the holiday. If you want to invest this year: a recipe for homemade puff pastry and 3 great filling options. Tiramisu with a fresh perspective has many diverse versions of tiramisu - the famous Italian dessert worldwide. This time I chose a recipe I first encountered in a training course in France, and since then I have made slight changes. For the shortcrust pastry (for a 27X9 cm pan): • 50 grams of cold butter • 100 grams of flour • 2 tablespoons of sugar (30 grams) • 1 egg yolk • 2 tablespoons of cold water For the coffee and milk chocolate cream: • 120 grams of milk (1/2 cup) • 125 ml of heavy cream (1/2 container) • 50 grams of roasted coffee beans • 1 tablespoon of instant coffee powder • 200 grams of milk chocolate • 1/2 packet of gelatin (7 grams) • 5 tablespoons of cold water For the cheese cream: • 175 ml of heavy cream (1/2 container) • 175 grams of mascarpone cheese (1/2 cup) • 30 grams of sugar (2 tablespoons) • 1/2 teaspoon of quality vanilla extract For the coating: • 100 grams of white chocolate • 100 grams of heavy cream • 5 grams of instant coffee • Chocolate sheets for decoration (optional) Start with the dough: In a food processor, mix cold butter and flour until crumbly. Add the sugar and mix slightly. Add the egg yolk and cold water and knead until a uniform, soft, and slightly sticky dough is formed. Flatten the dough between two sheets of baking paper into a rectangle 0.5 cm thick and 10X30 cm in size and freeze for half an hour. Remove, take off the top baking paper, and bake at 170 degrees for 12-15 minutes. Immediately after baking, while the dough is still warm, cut with a sharp knife into a rectangle measuring 27X9 cm. For the coffee cream: Soak the gelatin in water. Bring milk, cream, coffee beans, and instant coffee to a boil. Strain into a bowl with the chocolate. Mix, add the gelatin, and stir until uniform. It is recommended to finish with an immersion blender to achieve a smooth cream. Pour into a rectangular frame measuring 25X7 cm. Freeze for at least two hours. Whip all the cheese cream ingredients using a guitar whisk (recommended to finish with a hand mixer). Cover with cling film and refrigerate until use. Before assembling, whip to a stable whipped cream. For the coating: Boil the heavy cream, mix with the instant coffee, and finally with the white chocolate until a smooth mixture is obtained. Remove the frame containing the coffee and milk chocolate cream, coat with the topping, and place on the baked dough. Place circles of cheese cream on the coffee cream spaced 2-3 cm apart and decorate with light chocolate shards in between. To achieve smooth and shiny cheese cream circles, dip the spoon in boiling water, wipe with a paper towel, and immediately dip into the cheese cream to remove a circle. Repeat the process. You can also transfer to a piping bag and pipe the cream. You can also decorate on top with grated chocolate using a fine grater. For more food articles, recipes, and recommendations, visit our food section. Cold cheese and hot chocolate No-bake cheesecake with a perfect combination of chocolate flavors. This is a cake that is highly recommended to prepare a day before serving, so it sets properly. For the cake base (for a rectangular or round Pyrex pan with a diameter of 24 cm): • 1 egg white from an XL egg • 2 XL eggs at room temperature • 80 grams of sugar (1/2 cup minus 2 tablespoons) • 90 grams of flour (1/2 cup + 2 tablespoons) • 1/2 teaspoon of baking powder • 2 tablespoons of oil • 1/4 teaspoon of salt For the milk chocolate sauce: • 120 ml of milk (1/2 cup) • 60 ml of heavy cream 42% (1/4 cup) • 1 teaspoon of honey • 100 grams of chopped milk chocolate For the cheese whipped cream: • 400 ml of heavy cream (2/3 1 cups) • 180 ml of milk (3/4 cup) • 80 grams of sugar (1/3 cup + 1 tablespoon) • 80 grams of instant vanilla pudding (1 packet) • 500 grams of 5% white cheese For the topping: • 120 ml of heavy cream (1/2 cup) • 100 grams of milk chocolate Start with the base: Whip eggs, egg white, sugar, and a pinch of salt for about ten minutes until a light and very fluffy foam is obtained. Sift the flour and baking powder into a separate bowl and fold the flour mixture into the foam using a spatula, add the oil, and mix gently. Transfer to a pan lined with baking paper and greased on the edges. Bake for about 40 minutes at 160 degrees until the toothpick inserted comes out dry, cool completely, and slice the cake horizontally into two layers. For the chocolate sauce: Bring the milk, cream, and honey to a boil, pour over the chocolate, and stir until a smooth mixture is obtained. Wrap in cling film and cool. For the whipped cream: Whip cream, milk, and sugar to soft peaks. Lower the speed, add the vanilla pudding, and continue whipping for another minute. Transfer the cheese to a large bowl and gently fold in the whipped cream until a smooth and uniform mixture is obtained. Assembling: Place a layer of cake at the bottom of the pan and moisten with a third of the chocolate sauce. Immediately pour half of the cheese whipped cream and place another layer of cake on top, moisten with the remaining chocolate sauce, and pour the remaining cheese whipped cream. Wrap in cling film and transfer to the refrigerator for at least two hours. Topping: Bring the heavy cream to a boil, pour over the chocolate, and stir until a uniform and smooth mixture is obtained without air bubbles. Cover with cling film and let the topping cool at room temperature. Pour the topping over the cake and slice (you can also coat each slice separately at serving time. If the topping hardens slightly, heat it for a few seconds in the microwave). Keep for up to 4 days in the refrigerator. More in the food section: A wine review from the Vitkin winery, pioneers of the Mediterranean trend in local winemaking. Miki Shamo with 3 savory cheese pastries suitable for any event. Hila Alpert in a minute: This is how you raise (and cook!) organic chickens. Recommendation for Joya at the Cinematheque: Italian with a twist. Fraise - Tiramisu with mascarpone and strawberry. The cake consists of two layers of soft almond biscuit, mascarpone cream, and strawberry, and is decorated with a slightly browned meringue with a torch. For the soft almond biscuit (for a 24 cm diameter pan): • 2 egg whites • 1/3 cup of white sugar • 2 eggs • 2 egg yolks • 3/4 cup of demerara sugar (180 grams) • 220 grams of almond flour (2 cups minus 1 tablespoon) • 1/3 cup of sifted flour (50 grams) • 100 grams of melted butter For the amaretto mascarpone cream: • 375 grams of heavy cream • 250 grams of mascarpone cheese • 40 grams of amaretto liqueur • 50 grams of powdered sugar For the Italian meringue: • 3 egg whites • 180 grams of sugar (3/4 cup) • 60 grams of powdered sugar (1/2 cup) For decoration: • A basket of washed and dried strawberries (250 grams) Preheat the oven to 160 degrees. Whip egg whites and white sugar to a stable and smooth foam. In a bowl, mix demerara sugar, almond flour, flour, eggs, and egg yolks, add the butter, and mix until uniform. Fold the egg whites into the mixture. Pour into two baking pans and bake for about 25 minutes until golden. Whip all the cream ingredients at medium-high speed until a stable whipped cream is obtained. Transfer to a piping bag with a Saint-Honoré piping tip (available at specialty baking stores) and keep refrigerated. For the Italian meringue: Whip the egg whites and sugar until a stable foam is formed, then gently fold in the powdered sugar. Cut two almond biscuit rounds with a diameter of 24 cm. Place one biscuit round in the pan, pipe a thin layer of cream, arrange the strawberries in a single layer around the cake, and pipe the remaining cream on top. Place the second biscuit on top and freeze for one hour. Before serving, pipe the meringue on top and lightly brown it using a kitchen torch. Photography and styling: Itiel Zion, production: Sharon Shoval shemo1@bezeqint.net
Summary: The article presents three recipes for festive cheesecake for the holiday of Shavuot: Tiramisu, no-bake cheesecake, and Fraisier cake. The first recipe is Italian tiramisu with a coffee aroma, consisting of a crispy dough, coffee cream, milk chocolate, and cream cheese. The second recipe is for a no-bake cheesecake with a combination of chocolate flavors, consisting of a baked base, milk chocolate sauce, and cheese whipped cream. The third recipe is for a Fraisier cake - mascarpone and strawberry sponge, consisting of layers of almond biscuits, mascarpone cream, and strawberries, and decorated with meringue. The article is accompanied by photos illustrating the preparation steps for each cake.
Sentence in Summary: The third recipe is for a Fraisier cake - mascarpone and strawberry sponge, consisting of layers of almond biscuits, mascarpone cream, and strawberries, and decorated with meringue

Article: Was the mistress involved in the Watts family murder? Following the murder of the Watts family by the father that shocked the U.S. and made headlines again, holes in the mistress Nicole Kessinger's version are beginning to be revealed • Did she assist in the murder? In August 2018, the U.S. was shaken by the shocking murder case of an entire family. Chris Watts strangled his pregnant wife Shanann and their two young daughters Bella (4) and Celeste (3) to death with pillows he took from their bedroom. He buried his daughters in oil barrels and his wife in a shallow grave he dug. At first, he pretended to be devastated and even claimed that his family had been kidnapped (later he changed his story and claimed that his wife was the one who killed the girls), but later confessed to having an affair and committing the horrific murder. To avoid the death penalty, he agreed to a plea deal in which he was convicted of five counts of first-degree murder and sentenced to five life sentences. The mistress Nicole Kessinger and Chris Watts began their affair in June 2018. Two months later, as mentioned, Watts murdered his family. A writer named Chrissy Stockton is now claiming on the social media platform TikTok that Kessinger is far from being an innocent victim as she tried to present herself, and that her contribution to the heinous murder was much more significant than has been revealed so far. Stockton claims that the more she researched the details of the case in depth, the more information she discovered that revealed a completely different story than what was presented. According to her, Kessinger planned an insurance fraud, and along the way, the relationship that developed with Watts turned into an affair, but the ultimate goal was to get rid of his family so that the two could continue their relationship and collect her life insurance money. According to Stockton, there are too many holes in the story as it was presented, and she laid out many of them that arose from an in-depth investigation she conducted: "After failing a polygraph, Watts broke down pretty quickly and confessed to the heinous crime. If he was really a cold-blooded psychopathic killer, would he be in such a hurry to do it so quickly?" Stockton wondered. Kessinger claimed she did not know Watts was married because he did not wear a wedding ring. She also said that even when he admitted he was married and had a family, he insisted that he was separated. According to Kessinger, she discovered that his wife was pregnant and that they were still married only through news reports after the case exploded, but she frequently visited Shanann's Facebook page, where she updated Shanann about her pregnancy and family life. "In no way could it be understood from the posts that her marriage was close to its end," Stockton continued to argue. Additionally, Watts and Kessinger met at the site where he ultimately buried his wife and daughters. As a safety officer at the site, Kessinger had a lot of information about the location, including the fact that it would be perfect for committing an insurance fraud crime, one that would involve a powerful explosion that would destroy bodies and evidence. According to Kessinger, the first time she felt something was wrong was on the night of the murder. Before Shanann returned from her business trip that night, Watts and Kessinger had a long FaceTime conversation. Watts was lying on a mattress without bedding, and Kessinger claimed she thought it was strange since it was late at night. When the bodies of Watts' wife and daughters were discovered, they were wrapped in sheets. The FaceTime call between Watts and Kessinger lasted 111 minutes. Kessinger could not remember in her interrogation what they talked about, even though she was interrogated 72 hours after that call. The mistress also claimed that after she got the impression that Watts was too indifferent regarding his family's fate, she herself contacted the police. In fact, it was already after the police had been informed by the inspector at their work site (where he buried the bodies). Kessinger has no alibi for the time of the murder. Although their affair lasted only two months, the police discovered a wedding dress in Kessinger's possession. Additionally, her Google search history contained phrases like "marriage to your mistress" and "the man I'm having an affair with said he would leave his wife." The police revealed that Kessinger had searched for Watts' name on Google as early as August 2017, almost a year before the date she testified they first spoke, and months before she met him at work. On the morning of the murder, a neighbor testified that he saw a truck similar to the one owned by Kessinger outside Watts' house, and Stockton claims she encountered someone else on TikTok who analyzed the footage from the security cameras at his home. According to her, it is clearly visible there is someone who is not Watts, but it is hard to say for sure that it is Kessinger due to the low quality of the footage. On the other hand, that silhouette matches Kessinger's body structure, and there was also a wallet that matched the one the mistress was carrying at that time. Kessinger also tried to destroy her SIM card before handing it over to investigators and managed to delete text messages and photos of Watts from her phone's memory. Her search history indicates that she tried to find out if investigators could read messages she deleted. Her call history revealed that she was in contact with Watts on the night before the murder, the morning of the murder, and the day after. In her search history, it was found that Kessinger searched on Google for the value of Amber Frey, who was the mistress of Scott Peterson, who was accused of murdering his pregnant wife Laci Peterson, probably to find out how much money she earned from interviews and films produced about the murder. Throughout her interrogations, Kessinger avoided mentioning the names of Watts' wife and daughters, a well-known tactic of suspects in crimes trying to create a disconnection between themselves and their victims. Since Watts confessed to the heinous crime he committed, Kessinger has deleted all her social accounts, quit her job, and left her home. As far as is known, in the two years that have passed since, Kessinger has received numerous threats to her life and has become one of the most hated women in the country. According to rumors, due to the information she revealed in her interrogations, she was placed in a witness protection program. Earlier this month, she even filed an official request to change her name, all to be able to start a new chapter in her life and disconnect from the shocking case.
Summary: In August 2018, Chris Watts murdered his pregnant wife and two young daughters, initially claiming his innocence before confessing to the crime. Watts had an affair with Nichol Kessinger, who claimed she did not know about his family until after the murder. A writer named Chrissy Stockton alleges that Kessinger was more involved in the murder than is known, presenting evidence that supports her claim. Stockton points out holes in Kessinger's story, such as her frequent visits to Shanann Watts' Facebook page, where updates about the pregnancy and family were posted. Stockton argues that there is a lot of circumstantial evidence indicating Kessinger's involvement, including suspicious Google searches, mobile phone findings, and suspicious behavior. Kessinger was questioned by law enforcement but was never charged with any crime related to the murder. Since the murder, Kessinger has disappeared from public view and changed her name in an attempt to escape the scandal.
Sentence in Summary: In August 2018, Chris Watts murdered his pregnant wife and two young daughters, initially claiming his innocence before confessing to the crime
