Article: Beware of the children of the poor. He was a chubby child from a struggling family who didn't really understand what was happening in class. When his father asked him to study, "so you won't be like me," he decided to make a change. He lost 22 kg from his weight and became an outstanding student, in the army and at the Technion. Dr. Maor Fried from Ness Ziona will never forget that night at the age of 12. It was the night that changed his life: "I come from a struggling family, and my father worked three jobs. He would leave early, even before I woke up, and come home late, after I had gone to sleep." This is how Maor, the eldest of three brothers, recounts his story. "I waited until 11 at night for my father, who was absent for three weeks of work, and then he came home tired and worn out. He couldn't talk much and just told me, 'Son, study, so you can succeed and not be like me.'" Fried recounts with sparkling eyes that "I was a problematic student. The last one to grasp what the teacher was saying in class, but I decided to make a change. I told myself that I would no longer let my severe attention and concentration issues interfere with me, and I wouldn't cry about the situation at home. I would simply go conquer the world. I decided to make excellence a way of life, and so it was." As part of this new journey, Fried lost 22 kg from his weight, transforming from the worst student and the rejected child to an outstanding student, graduating with honors in a technological track at Ben Gurion High School in Ness Ziona. From there, he continued to the prestigious "Brakim" unit of the Intelligence Corps, where he completed an officer's course. "I wanted to combine excellence with Zionism and also contribute to the state," he says. He then continued to the Technion, where he completed his doctorate in 2017, at the age of 24, after only four years, and again with honors. He studied at the Faculty of Mechanical Engineering, specializing in applied mathematics. At the Technion, Maor also met the woman who became his wife. But this race did not focus him solely on himself. On the contrary, his success motivated him to help other teenagers make a change. He completed his doctorate in 2017, at the age of 24, after only four years, and again with honors. In his search for a contribution to society, he came to the "National Vision" initiative, which trains students for Zionist action, and the association's management mobilized to help him promote his initiative, which aims to assist all teenagers in Israel, especially those who struggle, to receive guidance, advancement, and professional advice along their path to excellence. He runs his initiative through an association he founded - "Learn to Succeed." "If I, with all my personal data and the difficult economic situation at home, succeeded - I believe that everyone can succeed. I promise to do everything to help every boy and girl realize this dream," says Dr. Maor Fried. It is hard to keep track of Fried's daily schedule as he runs around the clock: he is studying for a post-doctorate, serves as an external lecturer in mechanical engineering at Ben Gurion University, and works at the Ministry of Defense as an expert in earthquake assessments. "In my studies, I only received a partial scholarship and had to work around the clock in three jobs: private tutoring, cleaning, and waitressing. I slept four hours a night, and that's how I still do it today. It toughened me and gave me mental tools for excellence, success, and self-fulfillment, and that's why I decided that from now on, I would also contribute and help others," says Maor. "Anyone can" In the first stage, he began volunteering to teach young people in Haifa, Ness Ziona, Be'er Sheva, and Dimona. He focuses on struggling students from the end of elementary school, through middle school, to high school, mainly in at-risk youth. A direct result of this was the establishment of the 'Learn to Succeed' association, which currently has 34 active teachers, alongside 35 volunteers, some of whom are accountants and educators teaching dozens of students from the periphery, from low socio-economic backgrounds and at-risk youth in all subjects. "These studies mainly prepare them for life and give them tools to cope with difficulties. I want to shorten their path so they don't go through what I went through, and that's why I developed a new, unconventional teaching method," says Fried. "I arrived at a high school in Dimona to a class that was on a non-matriculation track - MABR. They told me they had no chance and that I should just minimize the damage. I told myself that this was unacceptable, that one could achieve more, and I really succeeded. I put in front of them salary tables of software engineers and told them they could earn 20,000 shekels a month, which is 4 ATVs. I showed them a film of 'Iron Dome,' which is the system that protects them, and they wanted to know what they needed to do to get there. I explained to them that they needed to study mathematics. That it wasn't a terrible waste of time as they had thought before, but it could really get them there. They understood and internalized it," he recounts. Learning to believe in yourself One of Maor's students in that class, in the robotics track, was Lior Satchkov, 18, from Dimona, now a soldier in the Air Force: "Maor was my mentor, and in the middle of 11th grade, I broke down from the pressure and difficulty of studies. I saw that I was really struggling with 5 units of mathematics, physics, and computer science, and my self-confidence really dropped after a few bad tests. I wanted to drop out of the track. Maor explained to me the importance of the track, gave me the tools to help myself and believe in my abilities, and only thanks to him did I continue studying in the track and finish with high grades," says Lior Satchkov. Some of Maor's students have become teachers in the project he manages, "Learn to Succeed." One of them is Shai Karichli, 28, currently a fourth-year student in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at Ben Gurion University. Shai grew up in the Florentin neighborhood in Tel Aviv, in a struggling family with immigrant parents without education, who worked hard to support him. Shai studied with Maor in a preparatory program and is now finishing mechanical engineering at the university and volunteering in the association as a mentor. "My goal is to give the youth who were like me the help that was given to me from a place of belief, through which I succeeded in my studies. I hope I can help them reach a better future," says Karichli. Eti Yitzhak is the director of the 'Pias Center' at the multidisciplinary school 'Zanman' in Dimona and a mentor of the robotics group, where Maor volunteered even before the 'Learn to Succeed' association was established: "We had the privilege that Maor was with us. The children here have one common denominator. Most of them had no dreams. The maximum they dreamed of was to be famous. Their self-esteem was low, and Maor was the responsible adult who propelled them to new heights with love and care. He mainly made them discover that they have the ability and skills needed to succeed on their own," attests Eti Yitzhak. The 'Learn to Succeed' association aims to reach hundreds of students each year, and therefore the association has approached the welfare and education departments in municipalities to try to raise resources and mentors. In addition, the association operates in several areas: producing YouTube videos about its activities; soon a book about the project and what stands behind it, written by Dr. Maor Fried, will be published; the association is establishing a hotline and a website available 24 hours a day, seven days a week, through which any teenager can ask and raise their difficulties and problems and receive immediate responses; recruiting students and mentors from the periphery who can share their personal stories with students and nurture self-confidence that will also propel them to excellence and success. What can you say to those who are experiencing difficulties, especially in their teenage years? "The last thing someone who is struggling should do is look for blame and complain that no one helped them. You shouldn't waste energy on negative things, but accept our situation as reality and focus on the positive. You should always ask yourself how to get the most out of yourself and improve the situation you are currently in. That's what I did, and that's what ultimately got me out of distress," says Fried. "My dream is to bring all our activities to academia. To attract struggling youth to universities and colleges so that they will be our students and later become mentors to others." As is customary today, Maor's initiative has turned to fundraising through the "Headstart" crowdfunding site: the goal is to raise 148,000 shekels to enable the activities of the "Learn to Succeed" association. So far, they have only raised 8,500 shekels from the first circle of friends, and now they hope for the participation of the masses. The donation address is www.lehatsliach.com
Summary: Dr. Maor Fried, who grew up in a struggling family, underwent a transformation in his life and went from a struggling student to an outstanding one in studies, the army, and academia. Fried, inspired by his personal story, founded the organization "Learn to Succeed" with the aim of helping teenagers, mainly from the periphery and low socio-economic backgrounds, achieve excellence. The organization, which employs teachers and volunteers, provides students with educational and value-based tools, with an emphasis on strengthening self-confidence. The story of Lior Sechkov, a student of Fried, demonstrates the impact of the organization on teenagers, as Lior almost dropped out of robotics studies but thanks to Fried's encouragement and support, he continued and successfully completed his studies. The organization is working to expand its activities through fundraising, producing films and a book, establishing a hotline and a website, and recruiting additional students and mentors. Fried emphasizes the importance of focusing on the positive and maximizing personal potential, and dreams of expanding the organization's activities into academia. The organization is working to raise donations to continue and promote its goals.

Article: 40 years since the Yom Kippur War: A tragedy of mistakes. Meir did not believe Sadat • Kissinger wanted the Egyptian president by his side • Both Kissinger and Meir doubted the ability of the strange president from Cairo to strive for peace. Three lions of American professional diplomacy gathered yesterday on the stage of the National Security Studies Institute at Tel Aviv University to analyze their policies in the years surrounding the Yom Kippur War, mainly leading up to it and shortly after. They were Dr. Harold Saunders and Professors William Quandt and Daniel Kurtzer. They did not come only as researchers in academia but also as witnesses. During those years, they held high positions in the National Security Council and the U.S. State Department, and were actually part of the close circle of aides to Henry Kissinger, who managed affairs from a different perspective than the Israeli leadership. Kissinger's America, under Richard Nixon, viewed the Israeli-Arab conflict as a secondary issue, a tributary to the great river of the Cold War with the Soviet Union. The generous armament to Israel, and more so the tacit agreement between Nixon and Golda Meir to avoid an Israeli withdrawal from Sinai - were primarily fueled by Kissinger's perception that this would convince Anwar Sadat that it is better to be an ally of Washington than a puppet of Moscow. The U.S. wanted to bring Egypt to its side, and in practice, it succeeded. Sadat expelled the Soviet advisors, but Washington slowly initiated the political process. There were two notable exceptions. One was hardly mentioned yesterday: at the end of the War of Attrition in the summer of 1970, Moshe Dayan initiated an interim agreement, according to which the IDF would withdraw from the Suez Canal to the Gidi and Mitla passes in Sinai. In return, Sadat would open the canal to shipping, and normalization would reduce the danger of war. Joseph Sisco explained to Ambassador Yitzhak Rabin that the proposal was a non-starter, as according to the American perception, an open canal for shipping would facilitate the Soviets in transporting equipment from the Bosporus Strait and the Mediterranean Sea through Suez to Vietnam, which harms Washington's interests. Only later did Kissinger change his mind and support it. In January 1971, Sadat surprised and agreed, but in Israel, Dayan and Rabin were few in their genuine support for the idea. Golda Meir and her ministers torpedoed it and missed the opportunity to prevent the Yom Kippur War. The last move was in 1973. The U.S. already felt that Sadat had abandoned the Soviets and turned to them, and they planned negotiations after the elections in Israel, and then the Egyptian president surprised by initiating the Yom Kippur War. At yesterday's symposium, the Americans said they were surprised that Sadat hurried to send softening explanations to Washington as to why he initiated an offensive move despite waiting for serious negotiations. At the end of the day, it was all a tragedy of mistakes. Meir did not believe Sadat. Kissinger wanted the Egyptian president by his side. Both Kissinger and Meir doubted the ability of the strange president from Cairo to strive for peace. When was the peace process initiated? Before Yom Kippur? Two years later, when Rabin, from his position as Prime Minister, effectively implemented the proposal he and Dayan had supported back in 1970? This is a matter for historians. Also for those who did the work and became historians. * * * Moshe Dayan: "This is the destruction of the third house"; Benny Peled: "I do not accept this". 40 years since the outbreak of the Yom Kippur War, the IDF Archive revealed recordings from the Air Force command bunker • In the face of Dayan's despair, a determined Air Force commander is heard: "We must destroy the bridges in the canal". 40 years since the outbreak of the Yom Kippur War, the IDF Archive in the Ministry of Defense published yesterday a transcript of six conversations held in the Air Force command bunker. In the conversations, one can hear the voices of then-Defense Minister Moshe Dayan, Chief of Staff Ra'al David (Dado) Elazar, Air Force Commander General Benny Peled, and Head of Intelligence in the Air Force Rafi Har-Lav, during the critical minutes of moving the forces of the corps from the southern front to the northern front - and in fact the launch of Operation "Model 5", the failed operation to eliminate the missile batteries in the Suez Canal area. During the transcripts, the grim picture presented by Minister Dayan was revealed, according to which Israel "is facing the destruction of the third house". The recordings begin about 15 hours after the outbreak of battles, at 05:00 on October 7, 1973. Around this time, Chief of Staff Elazar arrived at the bunker after the corps was required to change direction and concentrate its attacks in the northern sector instead of the southern sector. "You have a good connection with the Northern Command, right?" Dado is heard in the recordings. He receives a negative response. "Then connect with them. We need to help them because their situation is not good". The Chief of Staff is heard updating Air Force Commander Peled on the situation in the sector and telling him about his conversation with Northern Command General Yitzhak (Haka) Hofi. "He was very pessimistic... 'Listen, black tanks are pouring in'. Now I told him: 'Haka, the Air Force is going in there and finishing it'. So he said: 'That's fine, so are we'... Eight levels above, the morale, so as much as you can... In short, guys, we are now at the low point of the blockade and we are thirsty". Later, Peled is heard giving orders to his men to move their forces north. "Guys, on the Rapid route. Syrian tanks. There is not a single one of our tanks standing against them. The Rapid route goes down to the Jordan Valley, and if we do not stop them, they will go down to the Jordan Valley... Yes, so now the minister says that only the Air Force can stop them. So I ask, start moving forces there. Stop the patrols over the fields in the north and south. Come on, let them start moving everything to the Rapid route". "Defense of the Land of Israel". During these hours, the dilemma continued in the bunker: whether to go for an airstrike in Sinai (Tigris) or in the Golan Heights (Model)? "What can tip the scale is Model, and then a massive action. It can crush the Syrian army," estimates the Chief of Staff. "Model will take me four hours," Peled replies, "and I will not save the Golan Heights when the Syrian army has reached the Ramat Magshimim". After a brief conversation between the two generals, it was decided that in the end, the Air Force planes would go for an operation that would later be known as "Model 5", to eliminate the launchers of the Syrian army. "It's approved, go ahead," the Air Force commander urges his officers. Hours pass. The time is close to 11:00, Defense Minister Moshe Dayan arrives at the bunker again and reviews the situation. "Your plan to attack the Syrian missile array... is not yet in our hands. The Air Force saved one critical route, the Rapid route. But... the Syrians are pressing on other routes. The main force is retreating. Nefach is surrounded... there are endless Moroccans and Iraqis swarming there... there is a bottomless barrel there... half of our positions are surrounded. Nefach is surrounded... they are blocking all the routes...". To conclude, he tries to spur the officers: "This is the defense of the Land of Israel... this is what saves us". A few hours later, the Air Force commander is heard saying: "According to the opinion of the Defense Minister who returned from there (from the southern sector, to the 'Sh'), the situation according to his opinion is that we are quite close to the destruction of the third house... and even if Arik and Gorodish go for the night attack and they fail... then there is nothing between the canal and Tel Aviv that will stop. Now I do not accept this... now I see, to break the pessimism of these guys, that we need to destroy all the bridges on the canal. At all costs. Do you understand? This is the critical thing. If I can announce that there are no bridges in the canal, then they will be a little encouraged. They will be ready for a counterattack... so come on, I want to destroy the bridges, and it's not hard. It will take a few planes, but it needs to be done. Did you hear? Now I am leaving you. Do it." Lilach Shoval, our military affairs reporter.
Summary: At the conference marking 40 years since the Yom Kippur War, key figures from American diplomacy participated, including Dr. Harold Saunders, professors William Quandt and Daniel Kurtzer, who were close to Henry Kissinger. The United States, under the leadership of Kissinger and Richard Nixon, viewed the Israeli-Arab conflict as a secondary issue compared to the Cold War with the Soviet Union. The United States supported Israel in an effort to persuade Egypt to become its ally. During the War of Attrition, Moshe Dayan proposed an interim agreement that included an Israeli withdrawal and the opening of the Suez Canal, but the U.S. initially opposed it for fear of Soviet aid to Vietnam. Sadat agreed to the proposal in 1971, but Golda Meir and her ministers opposed it and missed the opportunity to prevent the Yom Kippur War. The U.S. was surprised by Sadat's decision to launch the Yom Kippur War, especially in light of his rapprochement with the West and negotiation plans. The Yom Kippur War is seen as a tragedy of mistakes, caused by mutual distrust and misunderstanding between the parties. The IDF archive revealed recordings from the Air Force command bunker during the war, which expose Moshe Dayan's despair in the face of Benny Peled's determination. In the recordings, Chief of Staff David Elazar can be heard instructing the Air Force to move forces north in light of the difficult situation on the Golan Heights. Dado describes the despair of the Northern Command General, Yitzhak Hofi, and emphasizes the urgent need for air support. Peled orders the transfer of all air forces to the Rafid axis, where Syrian forces threaten to break into the Jordan Valley. A difficult dilemma arose between an airstrike in Sinai (Tigris) and an attack on the Golan Heights (Dugman), ultimately deciding to launch Operation 'Dugman 5' to eliminate the Syrian missile batteries. Dayan, after returning from the southern front, expressed severe pessimism and claimed that Israel was facing 'the destruction of the third temple.' Peled, in contrast to Dayan's despair, remained determined and ordered the destruction of all bridges over the canal to encourage the forces and prevent Egyptian advancement.

"Narcos" Dutch version: The Moroccan drug lord who instills terror in the streets of Amsterdam

The assassination of journalist Peter de Vries revealed the extent of organized crime's control in the Netherlands over what happens in the country • Politicians in the Netherlands already understand that the struggle against immigrant gangs has been weak so far • "We were very naive," they admit in the country

The veteran and respected Dutch journalist, Peter de Vries, 64, died today (Thursday) from his injuries as a result of an assassination that took place about ten days ago. The assassination caused the usual shock ceremony in the country. Again, there was talk of an attempt by organized crime to harm Dutch democracy. Again, a cry arose against the underground world's undermining of the foundations of the rule of law in the Netherlands and the ongoing activities of drug trafficking gangs to turn the tolerant and liberal kingdom into a "Narco-State," whose economy is controlled by major drug dealers.

De Vries, who covered mainly the criminal field for decades, was shot in the evening in one of the streets of central Amsterdam, as he left a studio of the local RTL television station. De Vries was interviewed there about the largest trial ever held in the history of the Netherlands, which is currently ongoing - the trial of the biggest drug lord in the Netherlands, the Moroccan-Dutch criminal Ridwan Taghi. De Vries is one of the closest trusted individuals to the key witness in the trial, Nabil B., one of Taghi's criminal organization members who decided to surrender to the police in 2017 and became a state witness against his former boss while he was one of the most wanted criminals in the world and topped the list of the most wanted criminals in the Netherlands.

After the Amsterdam police announced Nabil B.'s defection, a campaign of murders, assassination attempts, threats of murder, and harassment began, of which the assassination of De Vries is just a part. The Dutch police have no doubt that the campaign is being managed under the personal command of Taghi. A week after the police announcement, Nabil's brother, a family man and manager of an advertising agency in northern Amsterdam, was murdered by an unknown assailant who came for a job interview and killed him with a direct shot to the back of his neck. In September 2019, Nabil's lawyer, Derk Wiersum, 44, was murdered in front of his wife outside their home in Amsterdam. Even then, the condemnations heard today about the "attack on civil society, democracy, and the rule of law" were echoed. De Vries indeed opposed the police's offer to provide him with close protection, knowing that he appeared on Taghi's hit list. However, proper police work could have prevented the assassination in advance. The Dutch police currently have two suspects in this assassination, and one of them - the sniper who carried out the shooting, has a direct personal connection to Taghi's close circle.

Three months after Wiersum's murder, in December 2019, Taghi was arrested following a comprehensive police operation in a luxurious villa in one of Dubai's upscale neighborhoods where he had lived for three years under a false name. For a long time, the Dutch police were at a loss: they had no idea where Taghi was staying. A reward of 100,000 euros, promised to anyone who would provide reliable information about the whereabouts of "Public Enemy Number One," was of no use. According to investigative sources, he rarely left the villa and was careful to maintain a low profile to avoid attracting attention.

In the large police operation to locate the wanted drug lord and arms trafficker, a hundred detectives participated, who managed through painstaking work to track Taghi's movements by following couriers and assistants who worked for him. When members of Dubai's internal security forces broke into his home, where he lived with his wife and six children, Taghi did not attempt to deny his true identity at all. However, he still denies all the charges against him today. Two international arrest warrants have been issued against him in recent years - by the authorities in the Netherlands, where he is suspected of involvement in 11 murders, and by the authorities in Morocco, where he is suspected of involvement in the murder of a senior judge's son in a shooting at a restaurant in the city of Marrakech. Despite the lack of a mutual extradition agreement between the United Arab Emirates and the Netherlands, Taghi was handed over to the Dutch, placed in custody, and put on trial along with 16 of his close aides in the largest criminal trial in the history of the Netherlands, which began in March 2020. Although he is under the heaviest security in prison, Taghi managed without much difficulty to organize the assassination attempt on De Vries's life, intended to instill fear in all those involved in the trial against the Moroccan mobster.

Shortly after Taghi's capture, his deputy - Said Razuki, also a Moroccan native, was captured in the Colombian "blow" of drug trafficking - the city of Medellin. Razuki was extradited to the Dutch authorities and is on trial alongside Taghi. All those on trial are accused of involvement in 13 "murder invitations," six of which resulted in the deaths of the targets. The murders and assassination attempts were carried out between 2015 and 2017 as part of a struggle for control over the cocaine trade market in the Netherlands. Whoever controls such a market in the Netherlands effectively controls one of the main transport arteries of drug trafficking in the world. There is no doubt that the success of the Dutch authorities in capturing the heads of Taghi's criminal organization in such a short time was made possible thanks to the information provided to them by Nabil B.

Taghi was born 43 years ago around the city of Tetouan in northern Morocco, an area considered one of the main drug smuggling routes from Africa to Europe. His parents immigrated to the Netherlands during the 1980s and settled in the city of Utrecht, where Ridwan grew up in a poor immigrant neighborhood. Even as a high school student, Taghi was involved in the local crime world, mainly in the sale of soft drugs - hashish and marijuana. This was a time when North African immigrants - mainly from Morocco, began to take over the drug trade in the Netherlands. In the language of the Moroccan community in the Netherlands, the local Moroccan crime world is called "Mocro-Mafia." Over the years, the Netherlands has become, due to its position as a major intercontinental crossroads in Europe and the authorities' openness to the consumption of soft drugs, a center for drug trafficking in Europe. Hard drugs arrive at Dutch ports, and from there they are funneled to the major markets in neighboring countries - France, Belgium, Germany, and Scandinavian countries. The largest synthetic drug manufacturing industry in Europe has also settled in the Netherlands. Taghi knew how to adapt to developments in the drug trade, dropped out of school, transitioned from soft drug trafficking to hard drug trafficking, mainly cocaine, took control of one of the main drug smuggling routes from Morocco to the Netherlands, and quickly became the number one drug lord in the Netherlands. Dutch police investigators estimate that Taghi heads a drug cartel that controls a third of all drug trafficking in Europe. He is also involved in arms trafficking. His personal fortune is estimated at around 100 million euros. The gang he leads is called "The Death Angels." And not without reason.

Brutal working methods allowed Taghi to establish his position at the top of the Dutch and European crime world. One of the people who worked with him testified: "He is a dangerous criminal, much more dangerous than many imagine." During the trial against him, the Dutch prosecution revealed text exchanges between Taghi and his associates. Taghi was asked, for example, how he felt after carrying out one of the murders he ordered. Taghi replied: "Hahaha, I'm already drunk, brother, and I need blood. Blood. Nothing else." "He eliminates people one after another, like falling dominoes," another close associate said about Taghi. "Taghi orders murders like others order coffee," it was said in one of the testimonies. Once the decision is made to eliminate someone, the orders are sent in code: "He needs to go," "Send him to sleep," "Give him a one-way ticket." The Dutch prosecution defined the criminal organization headed by Taghi as a "well-oiled killing machine." "Human life was not considered at all by the organization's members, given that they are attributed to two murders that were committed based on mistaken identity," it was stated in the prosecution's announcement. Taghi rarely trusts people, and once he has a suspicion about someone's loyalty, he quickly moves to "get rid" of the problem. One of the reasons Nabil B. decided, according to him, to switch sides was that Taghi began to threaten him that he would eliminate him. Nabil understood that only working with the police against the drug lord would give him any chance of staying alive. He just didn't anticipate that this would put all his close ones in the crosshairs.

The authorities in the Netherlands also estimate that Taghi offered his services to the Iranian regime for carrying out political assassinations of regime opponents in the Netherlands and across Europe. This cooperation is likely one of the reasons that prompted Taghi to move to Dubai, where he was captured: he could easily move between the Emirates and Iran. The Iranian regime has extensive access to the thriving drug trade operated by the terrorist organization "Hezbollah" from Lebanon and South America. Taghi's name is clearly linked to the assassination of Iranian regime opponent Mohammad Reza Kolahi Samadi, who was shot dead in the Netherlands in 2015. Samadi fled Iran in 1981 after being involved in a deadly attack against one of the centers of activity of the young Islamic Republic in Tehran - an attack in which over 70 people were killed, including someone who was considered at the time to be the second in command of the fresh regime, Ayatollah Mohammad Beheshti. Samadi found refuge in the Netherlands and lived there under a false name until he was located by agents of the Iranian regime and murdered. One of the detainees in this assassination case, the man who recruited the assassins and was sentenced to life imprisonment for it, was one of Taghi's men and received direct operational orders from him.

Will the trial of Taghi and his "Death Angels" allow the Netherlands to begin to reduce the influence of the crime world on the country? Since 2018, various authorities in the country have warned that the Netherlands is becoming a "Narco-State" - a state where the parallel economy of the underworld takes over the official economy, and where the crime world expands its influence into other areas of activity: media, government, justice, and police, and imposes corruption. Two years ago, dozens of Dutch police officers accused of collaborating with the underworld were suspended or fired, including a senior officer in the city of Utrecht who was on Taghi's payment list. According to Jan Struijs, chairman of the largest police union in the Netherlands, alarm bells have been ringing for some time, but politicians "were too naive" and did not act in time and as required. "Now, organized crime is eroding the foundations of our society," warns Struijs.
Summary: The "Death Angels" gang and its leader, Ridwan Taghi, control the world of organized crime in the Netherlands. Following the assassination of Dutch journalist Peter de Vries (64), which led to his death, the Dutch are concerned about the underground world undermining the foundations of the rule of law in the Netherlands. De Vries covered the criminal field, particularly the trial of Taghi, in which one of Taghi's senior former partners, Nabil B., serves as a state witness against him. From the moment Nabil defected from the organization in 2017, Taghi, according to the Dutch police, began a campaign of murders, attempted murders, and death threats: Nabil's brother was murdered a week after Nabil surrendered to the police, and in September 2019, Nabil's lawyer, Derek Wiersum, was murdered. In December 2019, Taghi was arrested in Dubai by the Dutch police after a large police operation to locate him involving 100 detectives. There are two arrest warrants against Taghi - one by the authorities in the Netherlands and the other by the authorities in Morocco. Taghi was transferred to custody in the Netherlands and was put on trial along with 16 of his aides. The trial began in March 2020. One of his aides being tried with him is his deputy, Said Razzouki, who was arrested in Medellin, Colombia, and extradited to the Netherlands. Taghi was born in the city of Tétouan in Morocco and immigrated with his parents to the Netherlands in the 1980s to the city of Utrecht. Even in high school, Taghi was involved in drug trafficking, and over the years he began to establish his position at the top of the Dutch criminal world. In the Netherlands, it is estimated that Taghi also offered his services to the Iranian regime for assistance in political assassinations of regime opponents, including Mohammad Reza Kolahi Samadi, who was murdered in 2015. As a result of this trial, the Netherlands hopes to reduce the influence of organized crime on the country.
Sentence in Summary: Following the assassination of Dutch journalist Peter de Vries (64), which led to his death, the Dutch are concerned about the underground world undermining the foundations of the rule of law in the Netherlands

Article:
Not just "Roi Boy": The child stars who got into trouble

Ofer Shechter, Michal HaKetana, Yuval HaMeBulBal, Jessica Biel, and more have found themselves in scandals while being considered stars among the younger generation • Please behave accordingly

The internet is buzzing following the exposure of an old video of the children's star "Roi Boy" - Roi Oz, in which he asks his children "Who wants to feed Bedouin kids?" The controversial behavior of someone who presents himself as a role model for children has once again raised the discussion about the gap that sometimes exists between the commercial image that is sold and marketed, and the real lives of those stars, a gap that sometimes leads to real damage to them and their fans alike. But "Roi Boy" is not the only children's star who has gotten into trouble over the years - here is the list of scandalous stars:

"Roi Boy"

"Roi Boy" // Photo: Danny Maron

The children's star "Roi Boy" (Roi Oz) caused a stir online when a video of him driving with his children in a car and encouraging them to feed Bedouin children cookies was circulated online. Although the video was filmed five years ago, it found its way to social media last weekend and managed to spark a storm online and beyond due to its racist and humiliating content, including a demand for a police investigation. After the uproar and the attack on him, Oz posted an apology: "In recent hours, a video from a private family trip five years ago has been circulating online, I want to express my deep sorrow for the things said in it, they are not worthy of being said. This is not my way of educating my children and it is not the way to educate any other child. My way is a way of love for humanity and I will continue to walk this path."

In 2016, Oz's name made headlines after it was reported that he threatened teenagers with a gun who were making noise outside his home. The police investigation revealed that he was not carrying a weapon and ultimately he even filed a counterclaim against the teenagers.

• Seen at a protest: "Roi Boy" on a horse

• "Roi Boy" is the one who filmed the racist video

Tal Mosseri

Tal Mosseri // Photo: Eric Sultan

The beloved children's star Tal Mosseri managed to cause nothing less than an earthquake in the sweet and innocent children's world when in May 2015 he announced on his Instagram that he was ending his journey on the children's channel after 18 years as a host. When it became clear that the reason was a move to Nickelodeon, Mosseri was dubbed a "traitor" and the internet was flooded with various posts, memes, and trolling comments about the move, including a Facebook group called "Victims of Tal Mosseri" that was opened.

• Watch: The connection between Tal Mosseri and Ortal Amar is revealed

Michal HaKetana and Miki Muchtar

Michal HaKetana, Miki Muchtar // Photo: Rafi Dluya

Two beloved children's stars, Michal HaKetana and Miki Muchtar, managed to anger quite a few fans (and parents) in January of this year when it was revealed that the hair they supposedly cut and donated for the "Braid of Strength" campaign in collaboration with the Zichron Menachem Association and Pantene was not actually theirs. The two were photographed holding a braid that was supposedly cut from their locks, but later the "media bluff" was revealed when they appeared with their original hair. After the exposure with us in "Israel Hayom", it was stated on behalf of the two that the issue is very important and close to their hearts and they attended the campaign launch event to raise awareness. They committed to cutting their real hair at the end of their show "Moteq Heroes of Dreams".

• Shock: The stars did not actually donate hair

Yuval HaMeBulBal

Yuval Shem Tov // Photo: Yossi Zaliger

The beloved children's star Yuval Shem Tov found himself in the headlines under criminal circumstances in 2019 when his name was linked to a high-profile drug case. Shem Tov was convicted of cocaine use as part of a plea deal, and he was sentenced to three months of probation, a fine of 2,500 shekels, and 300 hours of community service. During the sentencing arguments, Shem Tov asked to apologize and said: "I just want to take this opportunity to apologize to my parents, my wife, my children, and the audience for whom I am a role model. I take full responsibility for everything I have done."

• Yuval HaMeBulBal: "I just want to continue performing"

• Watch: Yuval HaMeBulBal comes to your home

Ofer Shechter

Ofer Shechter // Photo: Chino Paparazzi

Despite his innocent look, Ofer Shechter has been carrying the crown of the "bad boy" for years and manages to find himself in the headlines due to various provocations. The one that will probably be remembered for him forever is the scandal of the exposed buttocks at the beginning of his career as a host on the youth program "Exit" back in 2004. In the incident in question, Shechter exposed his buttocks to an audience of 20,000 teenagers at a performance. The provocative act caused such outrage that Shechter found himself being questioned under caution by the police and released on bail.

Jessica Biel

Jessica Biel // Photo: Reuters

In 2020, Jessica Biel starred in the successful series "Seventh Heaven", and that is roughly when the anger of many arose when she posed topless for "Gear" magazine at the age of 17. The producers of the series were so furious about the photos that they took legal action against the magazine. Biel later apologized and said she was misled and that the published photos were different from those agreed upon when she was on set.

Jamie Lynn Spears

Jamie Lynn Spears // Photo: Reuters

Britney's younger sister was at the center of a media and of course gossip storm when in 2007, at just 16 years old, she became pregnant. Jamie Lynn Spears starred at the time in Nickelodeon's youth series "Zoey 101". She did not return to appear in the series, and for years rumors in the industry claimed she was fired due to the pregnancy. About a year ago, Spears claimed to want to correct years of injustice to her image and revealed that the rumor about her firing was not true and that she left the series because her contract had expired.

Jennette McCurdy

Jennette McCurdy // Photo: Getty Images

Jennette McCurdy found herself in the headlines in 2014 when revealing selfies of her circulated online. At that time, Jennette starred alongside Ariana Grande in the youth series "Sam & Cat" on Nickelodeon. The series was not renewed for another season, and McCurdy did not hide her great anger at the network and the unprofessional treatment she received, as she claimed in tweets she posted. The actress boycotted the Kids' Choice Awards held that year and wrote on Reddit: "I am not a role model, I do not claim to be one, I am not trying to be a role model, and I do not want to be. Despite all the smiles and hearts, this is a world full of negativity and judgment."

When asked after the series ended if she would continue to work with Nickelodeon, she replied that she left the series to move on with her life. Regarding the claims she made against the network's treatment of her, she refused to elaborate but hinted that the treatment was hostile and particularly unfair compared to that received by her co-star Ariana Grande, with whom her relationship was also tense.

Taylor Momsen

Taylor Momsen // Photo: Getty Images

Taylor Momsen starred in the successful series "Gossip Girl", but one thing she apparently did not know was how to behave outside the set. In 2010, it was reported that the actress was fired from the series: according to reports, the firing came due to her wild behavior, which included particularly provocative clothing and conduct and unbearable behavior on set. Momsen founded the punk rock band The Pretty Reckless, which led to her transformation from a sweet girl to an angry rocker, including angry lyrics, heavy makeup, and provocative clothing.

Tim Gunn, the mentor of the reality show "Project Runway", who participated in one episode of "Gossip Girl", said that working with her on set was a nightmare and that she was a diva with pathetic and impossible behavior to work with. Momsen herself also spoke out against colleagues like Miley Cyrus, saying in an interview that she has no desire to be like the fame-chasing Cyrus and that she makes real music.

Samet was convicted of cocaine use as part of a plea deal, and he was sentenced to three months of probation, a fine of 2,500 shekels, and 300 hours of community service. During the sentencing arguments, Samet asked to apologize and said: "I just want to take this opportunity to apologize to my parents, my wife, my children, and the audience for whom I am a role model. I take full responsibility for everything I have done." Taylor Momsen starred in the successful series "Gossip Girl," but one thing she apparently did not know was how to behave off the set. In 2010, it was reported that the actress was fired from the series: according to reports, the firing came due to her wild behavior, which included particularly provocative clothing and conduct, and intolerable behavior on set. Momsen founded the punk rock band The Pretty Reckless, which led to her transformation from a sweet girl to an edgy rocker, including edgy lyrics, heavy makeup, and provocative clothing.

Britney's younger sister was at the center of a media and of course gossip storm when in 2007, at just 16 years old, she became pregnant. Jamie Lynn Spears starred at the time in Nickelodeon's youth series "Zoey 101". She did not return to appear in the series, and for years rumors in the industry claimed she was fired due to the pregnancy. About a year ago, Spears claimed to want to correct years of injustice to her image and revealed that the rumor about her firing was not true and that she left the series because her contract had expired.

Jennette McCurdy

Jennette McCurdy // Photo: Getty Images

Jennette McCurdy found herself in the headlines in 2014 when revealing selfies of her circulated online. At that time, Jennette starred alongside Ariana Grande in the youth series "Sam & Cat" on Nickelodeon. The series was not renewed for another season, and McCurdy did not hide her great anger at the network and the unprofessional treatment she received, as she claimed in tweets she posted. The actress boycotted the Kids' Choice Awards held that year and wrote on Reddit: "I am not a role model, I do not claim to be one, I am not trying to be a role model, and I do not want to be. Despite all the smiles and hearts, this is a world full of negativity and judgment."

When asked after the series ended if she would continue to work with Nickelodeon, she replied that she left the series to move on with her life. Regarding the claims she made against the network's treatment of her, she refused to elaborate but hinted that the treatment was hostile and particularly unfair compared to that received by her co-star Ariana Grande, with whom her relationship was also tense.

Taylor Momsen // Photo: Getty Images

Taylor Momsen starred in the successful series "Gossip Girl", but one thing she apparently did not know was how to behave outside the set. In 2010, it was reported that the actress was fired from the series: according to reports, the firing came due to her wild behavior, which included particularly provocative clothing and conduct and unbearable behavior on set. Momsen founded the punk rock band The Pretty Reckless, which led to her transformation from a sweet girl to an angry rocker, including angry lyrics, heavy makeup, and provocative clothing.

Tim Gunn, the mentor of the reality show "Project Runway", who participated in one episode of "Gossip Girl", said that working with her on set was a nightmare and that she was a diva with pathetic and impossible behavior to work with. Momsen herself also spoke out against colleagues like Miley Cyrus, saying in an interview that she has no desire to be like the fame-chasing Cyrus and that she makes real music.

Article:
Not just "Roi Boy": The child stars who got into trouble

Ofer Shechter, Michal HaKetana, Yuval HaMeBulBal, Jessica Biel, and more have found themselves in scandals while being considered stars among the younger generation • Please behave accordingly

The internet is buzzing following the exposure of an old video of the children's star "Roi Boy" - Roi Oz, in which he asks his children "Who wants to feed Bedouin kids?" The controversial behavior of someone who presents himself as a role model for children has once again raised the discussion about the gap that sometimes exists between the commercial image that is sold and marketed, and the real lives of those stars, a gap that sometimes leads to real damage to them and their fans alike. But "Roi Boy" is not the only children's star who has gotten into trouble over the years - here is the list of scandalous stars:

"Roi Boy"

"Roi Boy" // Photo: Danny Maron

The children's star "Roi Boy" (Roi Oz) caused a stir online when a video of him driving with his children in a car and encouraging them to feed Bedouin children cookies was circulated online. Although the video was filmed five years ago, it found its way to social media last weekend and managed to spark a storm online and beyond due to its racist and humiliating content, including a demand for a police investigation. After the uproar and the attack on him, Oz posted an apology: "In recent hours, a video from a private family trip five years ago has been circulating online, I want to express my deep sorrow for the things said in it, they are not worthy of being said. This is not my way of educating my children and it is not the way to educate any other child. My way is a way of love for humanity and I will continue to walk this path."

In 2016, Oz's name made headlines after it was reported that he threatened teenagers with a gun who were making noise outside his home. The police investigation revealed that he was not carrying a weapon and ultimately he even filed a counterclaim against the teenagers.

• Seen at a protest: "Roi Boy" on a horse

• "Roi Boy" is the one who filmed the racist video

Tal Mosseri

Tal Mosseri // Photo: Eric Sultan

The beloved children's star Tal Mosseri managed to cause nothing less than an earthquake in the sweet and innocent children's world when in May 2015 he announced on his Instagram that he was ending his journey on the children's channel after 18 years as a host. When it became clear that the reason was a move to Nickelodeon, Mosseri was dubbed a "traitor" and the internet was flooded with various posts, memes, and trolling comments about the move, including a Facebook group called "Victims of Tal Mosseri" that was opened.

• Watch: The connection between Tal Mosseri and Ortal Amar is revealed

Michal HaKetana and Miki Muchtar

Michal HaKetana, Miki Muchtar // Photo: Rafi Dluya

Two beloved children's stars, Michal HaKetana and Miki Muchtar, managed to anger quite a few fans (and parents) in January of this year when it was revealed that the hair they supposedly cut and donated for the "Braid of Strength" campaign in collaboration with the Zichron Menachem Association and Pantene was not actually theirs. The two were photographed holding a braid that was supposedly cut from their locks, but later the "media bluff" was revealed when they appeared with their original hair. After the exposure with us in "Israel Hayom", it was stated on behalf of the two that the issue is very important and close to their hearts and they attended the campaign launch event to raise awareness. They committed to cutting their real hair at the end of their show "Moteq Heroes of Dreams".

• Shock: The stars did not actually donate hair

Yuval HaMeBulBal

Yuval Shem Tov // Photo: Yossi Zaliger

The beloved children's star Yuval Shem Tov found himself in the headlines under criminal circumstances in 2019 when his name was linked to a high-profile drug case. Shem Tov was convicted of cocaine use as part of a plea deal, and he was sentenced to three months of probation, a fine of 2,500 shekels, and 300 hours of community service. During the sentencing arguments, Shem Tov asked to apologize and said: "I just want to take this opportunity to apologize to my parents, my wife, my children, and the audience for whom I am a role model. I take full responsibility for everything I have done."

• Yuval HaMeBulBal: "I just want to continue performing"

• Watch: Yuval HaMeBulBal comes to your home

Ofer Shechter

Ofer Shechter // Photo: Chino Paparazzi

Despite his innocent look, Ofer Shechter has been carrying the crown of the "bad boy" for years and manages to find himself in the headlines due to various provocations. The one that will probably be remembered for him forever is the scandal of the exposed buttocks at the beginning of his career as a host on the youth program "Exit" back in 2004. In the incident in question, Shechter exposed his buttocks to an audience of 20,000 teenagers at a performance. The provocative act caused such outrage that Shechter found himself being questioned under caution by the police and released on bail.

Jessica Biel

Jessica Biel // Photo: Reuters

In 2020, Jessica Biel starred in the successful series "Seventh Heaven", and that is roughly when the anger of many arose when she posed topless for "Gear" magazine at the age of 17. The producers of the series were so furious about the photos that they took legal action against the magazine. Article: The court disappointed: Yasmin's mother, may she rest in peace, left the country following the brutal death of Yasmin Vinta, may she rest in peace, and the exhausting handling of the court regarding the matter. Her mother, Dorina, decided to leave the country. A group of parents refuses to give up and fights for stricter punishment and supervision. The next demonstration will be on Wednesday. Last June, the entire country was shocked by the brutal death of Yasmin Vinta, may she rest in peace, a sweet baby just one year and two months old. According to suspicions, Yasmin was killed after Ina Skibanko, an assistant in the kindergarten where she was, wrapped a blanket around her and lay on her with her upper body for more than ten minutes. A little over two weeks ago, a small article in the newspaper revealed that 'the possibility is being examined that the caregiver accused of killing the toddler Yasmin Vinta, may she rest in peace, will be released to house arrest.' Yasmin's parents, who have since returned to Moldova, are exhausted from the legal delays and the discussions that are postponed time and again. They paid the highest price due to the negligence in the field and just want this not to happen to anyone else and that whoever took their daughter will be punished. 'After everything that happened to Yasmin, I went home, to Moldova,' Dorina, Yasmin's mother, told me yesterday. 'I wanted to be with my family and take care of myself. I am meeting with a psychologist who will help me find strength. I try to attend the court hearings, but it is difficult when the dates are constantly changed. I want all of this to be behind me; I have no more strength...' Vova and Dorina Vinta, along with Osnat, the case manager of Yasmin, may she rest in peace, is the most shocking case the country has known so far, but the struggle of Vova and Dorina Vinta, the parents of the poor baby, is unfortunately shared by many others who discovered that the people they trusted and entrusted their children to betrayed them and harmed their most precious ones. Yamit Abrahamov is a mother of twins who experienced abuse by a caregiver in the kindergarten they attended. She discovered this when her children were eight months old, after she hid recording devices in the area where they were: 'This is the most traumatic event of my life. I paid with my best money for a caregiver to provide warmth and love to my children while I had to go to work, and instead, they received screams, threats, curses, were locked in a room, and were not allowed to eat. I heard all of this through the recording devices. I will never forget the sound of the blows and the screams. I heard the caregiver calling my children 'scum', 'you will die', 'garbage', 'you will choke', and spreading frightening threats like 'I will come to break you.' 'The first thought that came to my mind was that I was going to kill her, but I composed myself and chose to trust the justice system to do justice. I transferred the materials to the police and was sure it would be handled. Instead, I was informed that the case was closed on the grounds of 'lack of evidence' because the recordings were illegal. It turns out that protecting the privacy of caregivers takes precedence over the safety of my children. Since then, I have not believed anyone anymore, I left my job and opened a kindergarten so I could keep a close eye on them.' Over 90% of cases where suspicions arise regarding the abuse of helpless toddlers, following which complaints were filed with the police and cases were opened, will be closed on the grounds of 'lack of public interest.' It is clear that there is great difficulty in proving the suspicions because children cannot testify, because the kindergarten areas are not filmed, or because the evidence was collected in an inadmissible manner (photos or wiretaps). But does this mean that we should take these cases lightly and continue to allow the same caregivers to work with children without any further in-depth checks, supervision, or criminal records? Photo: shutterstock Even in cases that lead to convictions, ridiculous sentences such as community service, fines, and in extreme cases, imprisonment for a few months are often received. The light sentences do not create any deterrence, and we parents do not even have the option to know that the caregiver who is trying so hard to endear herself to us may have been convicted in the past of violence against children. I have said this before and I will say it again: we have no way to ensure that the framework we chose for our young children is protected and safe. In fact, this unbearable reality turns us into gamblers. There is no mechanism that allows us to make informed decisions or a database that helps us know that we made the right choice. Ridiculous, but in this case, it is impossible to go 'safe' but only to gamble, hoping that we succeeded and found a place that will be safe for our children, and we can get them back at the end of the day healthy and whole in body and spirit. According to attorney Yaakov Shklar, who represents Yasmin Vinta's parents, may she rest in peace: 'The chilling crime case of the accused in this shocking case is a rare case even in our places known for violent crimes against the helpless. Anyone who watches the videos documenting the terrible actions of the accused will be struck with astonishment at the indifference and cruelty that arise from her actions. The court postponed the hearing to October 23 to examine a detention memorandum, which is intended to assess the danger posed by the accused and recommend an alternative to detention.' In order for the court not to disappoint this time, we need to act. I do not like to gamble, especially not when it comes to the safety of my children. It cannot be that from the moment maternity leave ends, the 'Russian roulette' begins, and I will walk around with great anxiety hoping that 'luck will be on my side' until my daughter turns three. It is time for the state to take responsibility and allow us to go safe and ensure the safety and security of our children. Photos from a demonstration for supervision. Photo: Osnat and the case managers Since Yasmin's death, a group of parents has been holding regular demonstrations so that this important issue does not fall off the agenda. On Wednesday, October 17, 2018, at 5:30 PM, the parents will demonstrate near the government complex in Tel Aviv, calling on the Minister of Justice to act for stricter punishment in every case of violence against the helpless. This is alongside the demand to legislate the supervision and regulation law and the camera law. I call on anyone who cares about their children's safety to come. As long as no approval is required to open a framework for children aged birth to three and there are no minimum conditions for the staff caring for our children, we will be exposed to more and more such cases, and unfortunately, we will not be able to prevent the next victims. Some of them, perhaps, we will never know about. For Dorina and Yamit, they had no idea that every morning they were bringing their most precious ones to this hell. For us parents, there is currently no mechanism that can guarantee that this will not happen to our children as well. The writer is the founder and leader of the 'Parenting by Right' movement, a lecturer, and the author of the book 'Naor the Hero.' The article was first published on the onlife website.
Summary: Dorina, the mother of Yasmin Vinta z"l, left the country following her daughter's death and the court's handling of the case. Yasmin's parents returned to Moldova, but they are still fighting for justice for their daughter. Yamit Abrahamov, a mother of twins, discovered through recording devices that the caregiver at the kindergarten was abusing her children. Despite the evidence, the case was closed due to "lack of evidence" and the caregiver was not prosecuted. Despite shocking cases of abuse, most cases are closed and the sentences for the convicted are low. The writer calls on more parents to join the fight for stricter legislation and closer supervision of early childhood frameworks. She emphasizes that the lack of oversight and enforcement exposes children to constant danger. The next demonstration will take place on Wednesday, 17.10.18, at 17:30 near the government complex in Tel Aviv. The goal is to demand that the Minister of Justice increase the penalties for violence against the vulnerable and promote legislation that will enhance supervision of early childhood frameworks. The writer, founder of the "Parenting by Right" movement, argues that the current situation turns parents into gamblers with their children's lives. She demands that the state take responsibility and allow parents to choose safe frameworks for their children. She emphasizes that without government action, tragic cases like the death of Yasmin Vinta z"l may repeat themselves.
Sentence in Summary: Yamit Abrahamov, a mother of twins, discovered through recording devices that the caregiver at the kindergarten was abusing her children
Best Match Sentences From Article: Yamit Abrahamov is a mother of twins who experienced abuse by a caregiver in the kindergarten they attended

Article: The Seder as it should be without matzo balls, beef roast, and coconut cake for dessert - this is not the Seder of the year, although I expect a Tel Aviv Passover Seder, but very traditional and classic. I smile to myself, thinking about the 14 little cousins who will gather from all corners of the sky to the holiday table. The youngest in the group will burst into childish and noisy singing and recite all the words of "What has changed," the teenagers will demonstrate fluent reading and will amuse the guests when they sing "Guy Guyainu" instead of "Day Dayenu," my Guy will blush with pleasure at the sound of the family Passover joke. The babies will gradually get used to the hustle and bustle, the joy, the sounds, and the melodies of a large, blessed, and united family, and I will seek to delight my loved ones through the pots and holiday delicacies. But since there is nothing new in these, I will still look to surprise, excite, and maintain the dignity and grandeur of tradition. In the clear and boiling chicken soup, matzo balls will swim, round and innocent-looking, those that have such rich and wonderful content - made of tender chicken pieces that have been cooked long in the soup. A magnificent beef roast will receive a smoky and deep aroma that will work wonders for the taste of the meat and also for the sweet root vegetables. And a chocolate coconut cake is almost a must, except that it is made with an abundance of coconut flakes, especially velvety and fragrant coconut oil, and dark bitter chocolate for color and taste. maya.darin.tlv@gmail.com Chicken soup with matzo balls ingredients (for 4 servings): for the soup: • 2 chicken thighs • 1 tablespoon canola oil • 1 onion, peeled and cut into strips • 1 celery root, peeled and cut into slices • 2 carrots, peeled and cut into round slices • A handful of parsley • A handful of dill • Salt to taste • Ground black pepper to taste for the matzo balls: • 1 cup matzo meal • Salt to taste • Ground black pepper to taste • 1 tablespoon sunflower oil • 1 1/3 cups boiling water • 2 eggs • A handful of parsley • A handful of dill • 1 cup chicken pieces from the soup preparation method: 1. Prepare the soup: Heat a pot, season the thighs with salt and ground black pepper, and brown on all sides. 2. Remove the thighs from the pot and set aside. 3. Add a tablespoon of canola oil to the pot and sauté the onion, celery root, and carrot until golden. 4. Return the thighs to the pot, pour in water to cover, add parsley and dill, and bring to a boil. 5. Lower the heat, cover with a lid, and simmer on low heat for about 45 minutes. 6. Adjust seasoning. 7. Prepare the matzo balls: Blend all the ingredients. Chill the mixture in the refrigerator for at least an hour. 8. Wet your hands and shape the mixture into balls the size of a walnut. Cook the balls in boiling salted water and serve with the soup. Beef roast in red wine ingredients (for 4 servings): • 6 slices of smoked goose breast • 1 kg beef roast, preferably shoulder • 2 tablespoons olive oil • 8 whole peeled garlic cloves • 4 whole carrots, peeled and halved lengthwise • 4 whole parsley roots, peeled and halved lengthwise • 2 onions, peeled and cut into strips • 2 cups red wine • 2 tablespoons silan (date syrup) • Salt to taste • Ground black pepper to taste • 1 cup fresh green peas preparation method: 1. Heat a wide pot suitable for roasting and serving. Brown the slices of goose breast, remove, and set aside. 2. Add oil, season the beef with salt and black pepper, and brown the piece on all sides. Remove and set aside. 3. Add the root vegetables and garlic cloves to the pot, sauté lightly, remove, and set aside. 4. Return the beef to the pot, arrange the goose breast, vegetables, and garlic around it, pour in the red wine and silan, and bring to a boil over high heat. Add water only to the edge of the piece, bring to a boil, lower the heat, cover with a lid, and simmer for about 3.5 hours. 5. Meanwhile, blanch the green peas in salted water, cool immediately, and peel off the transparent skin. 6. Cool the roast completely, remove the piece, and slice it thinly, return to the pot, and keep in the refrigerator until serving. 7. Before serving, reheat covered for about an hour and a half on low heat, adjust seasoning, and just before serving, add the green peas. Chocolate coconut cake ingredients (for a 28 cm diameter pan): • 200 grams chocolate broken into pieces • 200 grams solid coconut oil • 10 tablespoons powdered sugar • 6 separated eggs • 200 grams ground coconut • 1/2 cup cornstarch • 1 heaping teaspoon baking powder • 1 cup grated chocolate • Oil for greasing the pan for the topping: • 100 grams dark chocolate broken into pieces • 2 teaspoons solid coconut oil preparation method: 1. Preheat the oven to 180 degrees and line a pan with parchment paper and grease it. 2. Melt the chocolate and set aside. 3. In a food processor with a whisk, whip the coconut oil and 6 tablespoons of powdered sugar into a smooth paste. 4. Add the egg yolks one by one while continuing to whip. 5. Add the melted chocolate and continue to whip until a smooth and fluffy cream forms. 6. In the clean bowl of the food processor, whip the egg whites. When a thin foam forms, add 4 tablespoons of powdered sugar and continue to whip until a stable, white, and shiny foam forms. 7. Fold the chocolate cream into the foam, gently fold in the cornstarch and baking powder - then add the coconut and chocolate chips. 8. Pour into the pan and bake for about 30 minutes until golden. Cool. 9. Prepare the topping: Melt the chocolate and oil and stir until a shiny and uniform chocolate liquid is obtained. Coat the cake.
Summary: The writer is planning a traditional and classic Tel Aviv Passover Seder with her family. She looks forward to a special evening filled with singing, laughter, and shared enjoyment. She intends to prepare a festive and indulgent meal for her guests. In addition to classic dishes, she plans to surprise with new variations. The menu includes chicken soup with rich matzo balls. Additionally, there will be a deep-flavored beef roast served with root vegetables. For dessert, a rich and fragrant coconut chocolate cake will be served. The chicken soup recipe includes chicken thighs, root vegetables, and spices. The matzo balls will be made from matzo meal, cooked chicken pieces, and herbs. The roast will be prepared from a beef cut, smoked duck breast, red wine, and vegetables. Before serving, fresh green peas will be added to the roast. The coconut chocolate cake will use coconut oil, ground coconut, and dark chocolate. The cake will be topped with melted dark chocolate mixed with coconut oil. The recipes include detailed preparation instructions.
Sentence in Summary: The menu includes chicken soup with rich matzo balls
Best Match Sentences From Article: In the clear and boiling chicken soup, matzo balls will swim, round and innocent-looking, those that have such rich and wonderful content - made of tender chicken pieces that have been cooked long in the soup

Cool the roast completely, remove the piece, and slice it thinly, return to the pot, and keep in the refrigerator until serving. 7. Before serving, reheat covered for about an hour and a half on low heat, adjust seasoning, and just before serving, add the green peas. Chocolate coconut cake ingredients (for a 28 cm diameter pan): • 200 grams chocolate broken into pieces • 200 grams solid coconut oil • 10 tablespoons powdered sugar • 6 separated eggs • 200 grams ground coconut • 1/2 cup cornstarch • 1 heaping teaspoon baking powder • 1 cup grated chocolate • Oil for greasing the pan for the topping: • 100 grams dark chocolate broken into pieces • 2 teaspoons solid coconut oil preparation method: 1. Preheat the oven to 180 degrees and line a pan with parchment paper and grease it. 2. Melt the chocolate and set aside. 3. In a food processor with a whisk, whip the coconut oil and 6 tablespoons of powdered sugar into a smooth paste. 4. Add the egg yolks one by one while continuing to whip. 5. Add the melted chocolate and continue to whip until a smooth and fluffy cream forms. 6. In the clean bowl of the food processor, whip the egg whites. When a thin foam forms, add 4 tablespoons of powdered sugar and continue to whip until a stable, white, and shiny foam forms. 7. Fold the chocolate cream into the foam, gently fold in the cornstarch and baking powder - then add the coconut and chocolate chips. 8. Pour into the pan and bake for about 30 minutes until golden. Cool. 9. Prepare the topping: Melt the chocolate and oil and stir until a shiny and uniform chocolate liquid is obtained. Coat the cake.

Article:
Ran Baratz, advisor to Prime Minister Netanyahu, is ending his role

Baratz: "All the rumors about strained relations etc. are complete fabrications. Don't buy this used merchandise."

Ran Baratz, the senior advisor to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, announced yesterday that he is ending his role. Baratz, who took up his position only last summer as a communications advisor, wrote in a Facebook post: "About a month and a half ago, I spoke with Prime Minister Netanyahu and asked him to find a date for the end of my role. The reason is that I am far from realizing my potential in public relations, and I believe I have different advantages that will come to expression in other endeavors.

"The Prime Minister understood my feelings but asked me to stay in the role until I complete several tasks I started. It goes without saying that I happily agreed to the request. All the rumors about strained relations etc. are complete fabrications. Don't buy this used merchandise."

Over the weekend, Channel 2 commentator Amnon Abramovich reported that the Prime Minister's office is looking for a new spokesperson to replace Baratz.
Summary: Ran Baratz, the senior advisor to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, announced yesterday the end of his role. Baratz posted on Facebook that he asked PM Netanyahu to end his role because he feels he is far from fulfilling himself in communications, and the Prime Minister understood his feelings.
